channel.title: Residential Security by City on Close Protection and Security Services | CloseProtectionHire.com
channel.description: Recent content in Residential Security by City on Close Protection and Security Services | CloseProtectionHire.com

item.title: Residential Security in Alexandria, Egypt
item.description: Residential security in Alexandria splits along a fairly clear line: newer, business-district housing in Smouha and San Stefano, and older Corniche-facing buildings along the waterfront itself. The newer districts generally have conventional gated or concierge-level access already built in, and a survey there is mostly about confirming standards rather than uncovering gaps. Corniche properties are more variable, older buildings with a wide range of existing security infrastructure, sitting on a stretch of waterfront that never really empties of foot traffic, which changes what a meaningful security upgrade actually looks like from one building to the next.

item.title: Residential Security in Brasilia, Brazil
item.description: Ask a Brasilia superquadra resident what stands between their apartment and the street, and the answer usually involves a portaria, the building-level concierge and access-control desk found across Lucio Costa’s residential blocks, rather than a garden wall. That’s the starting point for residential security here: a genuinely different property typology from the standalone-house model common in Sao Paulo’s upmarket suburbs, and one that changes how a security plan gets built.

item.title: Residential Security in Canberra, Australia
item.description: Yarralumla and Forrest form the practical centre of residential security work in Canberra. These two suburbs hold the city’s largest concentration of embassy residences and senior-government housing, alongside neighbouring Barton and Deakin, and all four offer larger blocks and established gardens rather than the dense apartment stock typical of central Sydney or Melbourne. That geography, combined with generally low crime, makes conventional gating, perimeter lighting, and driveway access control straightforward to implement.

item.title: Residential Security in Chiang Mai, Thailand
item.description:
Chiang Mai has built one of Southeast Asia’s largest long-stay expatriate and remote-work communities, and residential security work here reflects that population more than it reflects any crime concern. Most requests involve condominiums and houses in Nimmanhaemin or the eastern suburbs, both well-infrastructured districts with an established international resident base and low pedestrian density outside peak hours.

The genuinely distinctive factor is air quality. During the 2026 burning season, coverage of the crisis recorded a PM2.5 reading of 188 micrograms per cubic metre on 29 March, more than four times the WHO’s 24-hour guideline, with northern Thai authorities logging over 2,090 fire hotspots in the first three months of the year. For a household planning to be in Chiang Mai from roughly mid-February through April, indoor air purifiers and PM2.5 sensors aren’t optional comfort items; they’re a standard part of the residential security brief here, alongside a ventilation and outdoor-activity plan built around daily air-quality forecasts.

item.title: Residential Security in Medan, Indonesia
item.description: Corporate and expat housing in Medan concentrates in two districts: Medan Polonia, built around the site of the city’s former airport, and the newer Setia Budi corridor further south. Both carry a well-established, comparatively low-risk residential profile, and both are the practical starting point for a household security survey here, whether the tenant is on a permanent posting or a project tied to North Sumatra’s palm oil and agribusiness sector.

item.title: Residential Security in Pretoria, South Africa
item.description: Waterkloof and Brooklyn hold most of Pretoria’s diplomatic community and, with it, the city’s most developed residential-security infrastructure: neighbourhood watch structures, estate-level access control in newer developments, and a private-patrol presence that reflects the concentration of embassies and senior corporate households in these suburbs. That infrastructure is well established, but it exists because of the underlying risk, not despite it. Gauteng province, which contains Pretoria, records a disproportionate share of South Africa’s carjackings and residential crime, and security planning here starts from that provincial baseline.

item.title: Residential Security in Rabat, Morocco
item.description:
Rabat’s residential security picture starts with a fact that shapes almost everything else: this is the seat of government, and its most secured residential district, Souissi, is also its diplomatic quarter. A large share of the households needing property security here are diplomatic postings or government-linked corporate roles rather than the finance-sector relocations that dominate Casablanca’s market.

That changes the practical work. Souissi properties often sit near, or effectively within, a broader zone of state security presence built around the embassies themselves, which is a genuine asset for perimeter planning, but only if it’s formally coordinated rather than assumed to extend automatically to a private residence next door. Agdal and the newer Hay Riad development offer a calmer, more conventional residential security picture: standard gating, perimeter walls, and controlled access, well suited to family accommodation.

item.title: Residential Security in Saint Petersburg
item.description: This page is for a narrow readership: the small number of people who maintain a genuinely necessary residential presence in Saint Petersburg despite the FCDO’s advisory against all travel to Russia. It is not written to encourage anyone toward relocation, and if you are weighing a move to the city for any reason other than an obligation you cannot defer, the advisory itself is the first document to read, not this one.

item.title: Residential Security in Sharjah, UAE
item.description:
A lot of Sharjah’s residential security work starts with a simple fact: it’s cheaper to live here than in Dubai, and a large share of Dubai’s corporate workforce takes advantage of that by commuting daily across the Al Ittihad Road border. Companies relocating staff into Sharjah housing for exactly this reason need a property review that accounts for the commute as much as the property itself.

The other factor that genuinely changes a Sharjah residential plan is the legal environment. Sharjah bans alcohol completely, the only UAE emirate to do so, and that applies inside private homes, not just in public venues. Domestic staff vetting here goes slightly further than the standard UAE checks: identity verification, residency status, and employer references, plus a clear briefing that Sharjah’s rules, including the Decency and Public Conduct Rules in force since 2001, differ from what staff may have experienced in a Dubai household.

item.title: Residential Security in Valparaiso, Chile
item.description: Residential security in Valparaiso starts from a fact that shapes almost everything else: the city sits on steep hillsides in one of the most seismically active regions on earth, with a documented history of major earthquakes including significant events in 1906 and 1985. That history turns structural anchoring, confirmed egress routes, and a rehearsed household emergency plan into standard components of a Valparaiso residential survey rather than optional extras bolted on afterwards.

item.title: Residential Security in Aarhus, Denmark
item.description:
Maersk, Vestas, Arla Foods, Salling Group, and Jysk are all headquartered in Aarhus, and between them they bring a steady flow of relocating executives to Denmark’s second-largest city. Add a technology and life-sciences cluster forming around Katrinebjerg and the Agro Food Park, and the residential security picture in Aarhus is shaped almost entirely by where these employers and Aarhus University, with its 44,000-plus students, sit relative to the harbour.

Aarhus C, the business core around the harbour, and Aarhus Ø, the newer harbourfront development, are both well-suited to executive and family accommodation: modern stock, straightforward access control, and a police presence that matches the city’s low overall risk profile. Gellerup, in the Brabrand district, is the one area to flag rather than consider: it has featured on Denmark’s official disadvantaged-area list since 2014, with a partial relisting in 2017, and sits well outside the business and university areas that most relocating families use day to day.

item.title: Residential Security in Charleroi, Belgium
item.description:
Charleroi carries a reputation as one of Belgium’s higher-crime cities, a legacy of its coal and steel past and a regional unemployment rate, 7.8% in Wallonia in 2025 against a national figure of 6.2% per Statbel, that still runs above the country average. It’s a reputation worth taking seriously in relocation planning, but not one that rules the whole city out.

Gosselies is the area doing the most to change that picture. Sonaca’s aerospace-structures campus and the A6K digital-innovation hub, backed by EU Recovery and Resilience Plan funding and scaling toward 10,000 learners a year from autumn 2026, represent genuine redevelopment, and while Gosselies itself is primarily a business and campus district, the areas around it carry a materially calmer profile than the city centre and are the natural draw for relocating Sonaca or A6K-linked families.

item.title: Residential Security in Cordoba, Spain
item.description:
Cordoba province holds Spain’s national temperature record, 47.4C recorded at La Rambla on 14 August 2021, according to AEMET, and that figure shapes residential security planning here more directly than in most Spanish cities: verified air conditioning and shaded garden space are safety items, not upgrades, for a family moving in over the summer months.

Corporate relocation to Cordoba is modest next to Madrid or Barcelona, weighted toward the agricultural sector and the oleotourism circuit built on Andalusia’s olive-oil economy, so residential security work here more often covers extended-stay or project-based accommodation than a permanent family move. Ronda de los Tejares and the modern business district north of the historic centre suit that pattern well: low pedestrian density outside festival dates, straightforward access control, and consistent policing.

item.title: Residential Security in Gdansk, Poland
item.description:
Gdansk’s identity runs through its port, and residential security work here reflects that directly: the shipping sector and the amber trade, including the Amberif and Ambermart trade fairs built on the historic Amber Road, are what bring most relocating families to the city, alongside a smaller institutional and diplomatic community tied to the European Solidarity Centre.

Oliwa and Wrzeszcz are where those families settle: residential and university districts with a lower crime profile than the tourist core, popular for extended corporate accommodation. Dlugi Targ and the Motlawa riverfront, by contrast, are well-policed but carry tourist-crowd density at all hours, sharpest during cruise-ship arrivals, which makes the old town a better place to visit than to live.

item.title: Residential Security in Hannover, Germany
item.description:
Continental AG’s headquarters opened in its current Continental-Plaza form in December 2023, and it anchors a good part of Hannover’s residential security work: a modern, low-crime corporate campus in the southern business district that draws relocating executive families almost by default.

List and Waldhausen are the established alternative, residential districts long favoured by expatriate and corporate accommodation with a consistently low crime profile. Wolfsburg, home to Volkswagen Group’s headquarters, sits around 90km away and roughly 30 minutes off by ICE train, close enough that some relocating families treat it as within commuting range rather than a separate city.

item.title: Residential Security in Katowice, Poland
item.description:
Katowice’s Special Economic Zone, established in 1996, has drawn more than PLN 50 billion in investment and created over 100,000 jobs, and fDi Intelligence named it Europe’s best SEZ for 2024. Business-services employment has grown by almost 70% over four years to around 27,000 workers, and that growth is the main reason families relocate here now, a genuine shift from the city’s Upper Silesia coal-mining and heavy-industry roots.

Ligota and Panewniki are the residential answer to that growth: quieter districts with a lower crime profile than the centre, favoured for extended corporate accommodation. The city centre business district around the Spodek arena and MCK congress centre is modern and well-maintained, heavily used for conferences and corporate events, but it’s a commercial rather than residential setting.

item.title: Residential Security in Namur, Belgium
item.description:
Namur has been the official capital of Wallonia since 1986, confirmed in 2010, and is home to the Parliament of Wallonia and the Government of Wallonia at the Elysette. That gives the city a relocating population genuinely different from most Belgian cities: political, institutional, and diplomatic staff rather than the corporate transfers that dominate elsewhere.

The confluence district, where the Meuse and Sambre rivers meet beneath the Citadel of Namur, and the government quarter around the Parliament and the Elysette are well-maintained, with a strong police presence during government sitting weeks that carries over to the adjacent residential streets. Namur’s 2023 L’Avenir local security-perception survey backs up the calmer picture: residents’ top concerns were road-user behaviour at 51%, public-space nuisance at 35%, and criminal facts at 34%, a perception profile a long way from the reputation of a former heavy-industry basin.

item.title: Residential Security in Nuremberg, Germany
item.description:
Fair weeks define parts of the Nuremberg calendar the way port schedules define Hamburg’s: Spielwarenmesse in late January and BrauBeviale in November both bring severe citywide hotel and transfer scarcity, a detail worth knowing even for residents, since it shapes how any household hosting visiting business guests needs to plan.

Gostenhof and St. Johannis, mixed residential and commercial districts west of the old town, are where most relocating families land: lower footfall than the fair-week centre, a settled profile, and straightforward access to the rest of the city. Many of these households connect to the trade-fair sector directly, or commute toward Herzogenaurach for Adidas or Puma, or into the wider region for Siemens.

item.title: Residential Security in Rouen, France
item.description:
French Ministry of Interior data published in April 2024 ranked Rouen 4th of 368 French cities over 22,500 residents for recorded crime per capita, ahead of both Lyon and Paris, though the Ministry’s own methodology describes the figure as informational rather than a full risk assessment. It’s a genuinely relevant number for a family choosing a Rouen neighbourhood, more so than for most French regional-capital relocations.

Rive Gauche, the Left Bank business district, is the practical answer: modern development, lower ambient crime than the historic core, and normal vehicle access that Vieux Rouen’s pedestrianised streets around the Cathedral and Gros-Horloge simply don’t offer. The old town is well-policed and pleasant during business hours, but it suits short stays better than a family home.

item.title: Residential Security in Wroclaw, Poland
item.description: KPMG Poland counted 234 modern business-service centres operating in Wroclaw as of May 2025, Google, HP, Nokia, IBM, UBS, and Volvo IT among them, and that footprint is what brings most relocating families to the city. It isn’t a fixed number: UBS’s local headcount fell from around 2,200 to somewhere between 1,400 and 1,600 by the third quarter of 2024 amid sector restructuring, a reminder that this employment base moves with global business cycles.

item.title: Residential Security in Antwerp, Belgium
item.description: Antwerp is a working port city with a diamond trade woven into its wealthier districts, and residential security planning there tends to follow that duality: quiet, settled streets a short walk from areas that see genuinely elevated footfall-driven crime. Zurenborg, with its Belle Epoque terraces and long-established expatriate community, and neighbouring Berchem are where most executive and HNWI households land, and both carry a materially lower baseline than the area around Antwerpen-Centraal.

item.title: Residential Security in Bremen, Germany
item.description: Bremen is a city-state built around its port and, more recently, its aerospace sector, and its residential security work reflects a straightforward split: quiet eastern districts for accommodation, and a couple of specific areas to be aware of rather than live in. Schwachhausen is the district most executive families land in, an affluent quarter with good infrastructure and a police presence that reads as consistent rather than reactive. Horn-Lehe, further out near the university, offers a similarly settled but more modest alternative.

item.title: Residential Security in Dusseldorf, Germany
item.description:
Dusseldorf sits on the Rhine as a corporate and fashion-retail hub, and its residential security pattern follows the river closely. Oberkassel, on the left bank, is the polished end of the market: elegant streets, high-end shopping, and a police presence that keeps the area feeling settled rather than watched. Kaiserswerth, further upstream, offers a historic-village alternative with regular patrols and a genuinely quieter pace.

The city’s Japanese business community, centred on Immermannstrasse and known locally as Little Tokyo, means a steady share of residential security work here involves relocating expatriate families, often coordinated with the same relocation agents handling schools and visas. Perimeter planning differs sharply by district: Oberkassel is mostly apartment stock, so building-entry security matters more than individual fencing, while Kaiserswerth’s standalone houses take a more conventional gate-and-boundary approach. Two districts are worth avoiding for accommodation rather than merely noting: the Hauptbahnhof surrounds and the Altstadt, both of which see documented disorder once the bars fill up.

item.title: Residential Security in Leipzig, Germany
item.description: Leipzig’s growth as an automotive manufacturing centre, anchored by BMW and Porsche plants on the city’s edge, has brought a steady flow of relocating families whose residential security needs look fairly consistent: quiet western or northern districts, a fixed relocation date, and staff vetting that has to keep pace. The Waldstrassenviertel, a Wilhelminian-era quarter popular with expatriate professionals, and Gohlis, further north with better transport and a lower crime baseline, are where most of that demand lands.

item.title: Residential Security in Liege, Belgium
item.description: Liege carries a heavier industrial legacy than most Belgian cities its size, and that shows in a documented crime rate above the national urban average, a fact worth stating plainly rather than glossing over. It does not mean the whole city is unsuitable for residential life. Cointe, on its hillside south of the centre, and Angleur, along the Meuse with good access to the E25, are both quieter districts that support conventional family accommodation without the elevated profile seen closer in.

item.title: Residential Security in Lille, France
item.description:
Lille sits close enough to the Belgian border that its residential security pattern draws comparisons with cities like Brussels, but its own retail-sector economy, anchored by Auchan and Decathlon’s nearby headquarters, gives it a distinct client base. Vieux-Lille, the cobblestoned historic quarter, and Vauban-Esquermes, bordering the Citadelle parkland and served well by tram, are where most of that demand settles, and both carry a low, settled crime profile.

The exceptions are specific and officially documented rather than a matter of local reputation. Lille-Sud and Moulins are designated quartiers prioritaires by the Prefecture du Nord, with confirmed drug dealing, and neither features in any residential recommendation regardless of budget. Vieux-Lille’s narrow streets constrain what a perimeter upgrade can look like; heritage frontages rule out anything too visible, so building-level access control tends to carry more weight there than in Vauban-Esquermes, where individual townhouse plots allow a more conventional gate-and-alarm setup.

item.title: Residential Security in Montpellier, France
item.description: Montpellier has built a reputation as one of France’s faster-growing tech and biotech hubs, and its residential security profile reads accordingly: a modern planned district, an older calmer quarter, and one clearly designated area to avoid. Antigone, laid out east of the historic Ecusson with wide public spaces, and Beaux-Arts and Boutonnet, adjoining the old centre, are where most relocating professionals settle, and both sit comfortably below the city’s higher-risk areas.

item.title: Residential Security in Palma de Mallorca, Spain
item.description:
Palma de Mallorca runs two distinct residential worlds side by side: the gated hillside estates of Son Vida, long the established address for HNWI and executive families, and a livelier seafront scene where Portixol offers a quieter, walkable alternative closer to the water. Son Vida’s own private security and controlled entry points mean a household survey there is really about the second layer of protection, not the first.

The superyacht economy shapes a good share of demand here. Real Club Nautico de Palma and STP Shipyard Palma between them draw owners who split their time between a vessel and a shore residence, and their vetting and monitoring needs often track the sailing season rather than a fixed calendar. Playa de Palma and s’Arenal, the dense nightlife strip straddling the Palma and Llucmajor boundary, sit at the opposite end of the spectrum from Son Vida: elevated disorder after dark, and firmly outside any residential recommendation.

item.title: Residential Security in Strasbourg, France
item.description: Strasbourg’s identity as a seat of European institutions gives its residential security work a distinct rhythm, one tied to the parliamentary calendar as much as to the property itself. Families connected to the European Parliament, the Council of Europe, or the European Pharmacopoeia move in and out of the city on a schedule, and Neustadt and Orangerie, the wide-boulevarded imperial-period district, along with the historic canal quarter of Petite France, are where most of them settle.

item.title: Residential Security in Stuttgart, Germany
item.description:
Stuttgart’s residential security demand tracks its two flagship automotive employers closely: Mercedes-Benz at Untertruckheim and Porsche at Zuffenhausen between them account for a large share of the executive and expatriate families choosing Killesberg or Degerloch as a base. Killesberg’s park-side setting and Degerloch’s hillside quiet both carry a genuinely low crime baseline, well below the city average.

There are two situations worth flagging without overstating them. The Hauptbahnhof concourse has seen documented pickpocketing linked to the ongoing Stuttgart 21 construction disruption, and Bad Cannstatt, home to the Cannstatter Wasen fairground, sees a real rise in theft and disorder during the Cannstatter Volksfest and Fruhlingsfest festival periods. Neither reflects a residential-district risk; they are date- and location-specific and matter mainly for planning household movement around those events.

item.title: Residential Security in Bologna, Italy
item.description: Bologna’s residential security picture is shaped less by violent crime, which FCDO Italy 2024 guidance rates low across the country, and more by the practical realities of a historic city: shared stairwells, porticoed apartment blocks, and a trade-fair calendar that periodically fills the streets around BolognaFiere with visitors. A survey for a centre-city apartment near Piazza Maggiore looks quite different from one for a house in the quieter residential streets near the exhibition grounds, and the recommendations that follow reflect that.

item.title: Residential Security in Bordeaux, France
item.description: Bordeaux’s residential security work splits between two distinct settings: the city itself, with its Chartrons merchant district, Place de la Bourse, and the Merignac aerospace and defence cluster nearby, and the wider wine region, where chateau properties across the Medoc, Pauillac, and Saint-Emilion circuit combine a private residence with working vineyard grounds. France’s Vigipirate Urgence Attentat alert applies nationally, and locally the more relevant pattern is demonstrations concentrated at Place de la Victoire and Cours de l’Intendance, a public-order issue rather than a residential one.

item.title: Residential Security in Eindhoven, Netherlands
item.description: Eindhoven’s residential security profile is unusual among European tech cities because the relevant risk is named explicitly by a national intelligence service. The AIVD’s 2023 annual report identifies the Dutch semiconductor and high-tech sector, ASML (whose campus sits in neighbouring Veldhoven) and NXP Semiconductors among them, as a target of state-actor corporate espionage. That is primarily a corporate and information-security matter, but it changes how a residential security assessment is approached for households connected to the sector, particularly around staff vetting and home network security rather than physical intrusion alone.

item.title: Residential Security in Genoa, Italy
item.description: Genoa presents an unusual mix for residential security planning: a medieval old town whose narrow Caruggi alleys were never designed for vehicles, a working port with its own maritime business community, and hillside suburbs where executives connected to shipping and logistics firms often settle. FCDO Italy 2024 guidance rates the country low risk overall, and Genoa’s documented crime pattern sits mainly in petty theft around busier transit points such as Brignole station and Piazza de Ferrari rather than in residential burglary.

item.title: Residential Security in Ghent, Belgium
item.description: Ghent’s residential security picture is defined by contrast between its historic core and its industrial edge. The Graslei and Korenmarkt waterfront, along with the Overpoort student nightlife area, show a documented pattern of opportunistic crime in Federale Politie data, worse during festival periods such as the Gentse Feesten, while the Ghent-Terneuzen Canal industrial zone, home to employers such as ArcelorMittal and Volvo Cars Belgium, presents an entirely different set of considerations centred on nearby commercial traffic rather than crime concentration.

item.title: Residential Security in Malaga, Spain
item.description: Malaga’s residential security demand splits fairly cleanly into two profiles. There is the compact, walkable city centre around Calle Larios, the Alcazaba, and the Muelle Uno waterfront, where apartment living dominates and shared building entrances are the norm. Then there is the Costa del Sol corridor running west toward Marbella along the AP-7 motorway, home to a concentration of villa properties with private grounds, pools, and grounds staff. FCDO guidance for Spain describes opportunistic theft in the busier tourist areas rather than a targeted residential crime pattern, but the two property types still call for different survey work.

item.title: Residential Security in Nantes, France
item.description: Nantes sits at the crossing point of several distinct employer sectors, and residential security planning in the city reflects that spread. The aerospace and defence cluster at Bouguenais, anchored by Airbus and Thales, draws a steady flow of relocating executives into the residential districts near the airport, while the naval and offshore-energy sector based around the Chantiers de l’Atlantique shipyard in Saint-Nazaire, roughly 60km west via the N165, brings a separate set of considerations tied to a more industrial coastal setting. France’s Vigipirate Urgence Attentat alert applies nationally, with the more locally relevant pattern being demonstrations at Place du Commerce and Cours des 50-Otages during pension-reform protests.

item.title: Residential Security in Rennes, France
item.description: Rennes combines a compact historic centre with a fast-growing tech corridor at Beaulieu and Atalante, and residential security planning in the city reflects both. France’s national Vigipirate Urgence Attentat alert level applies uniformly, with no specific elevation for Rennes; what actually shapes day-to-day planning locally is the recurring pattern of demonstrations at Place de la Republique and Place du Parlement over pension reform and agricultural policy, which can affect street access on a given day without pointing to any residential crime concern.

item.title: Residential Security in Utrecht, Netherlands
item.description: Utrecht’s residential security picture is shaped by two features of the city: Utrecht Centraal, the busiest rail station in the Netherlands, with a documented pickpocketing pattern in the station and adjoining Hoog Catharijne shopping complex, and a concentration of financial and insurance-sector employers, including Rabobank, a.s.r., and Triodos Bank, whose relocating executives often settle in the residential suburbs or canal-belt apartments around the Oudegracht. The station-area crime pattern is a transit and retail issue rather than a residential one, and a property assessment treats the two separately.

item.title: Residential Security in Zaragoza, Spain
item.description: Zaragoza’s residential security profile is tied closely to how the city works: a historic centre around the Basilica del Pilar and El Tubo that draws visitors and the associated petty-crime pattern, an automotive sector anchored by the Stellantis plant at Figueruelas, and PLAZA, one of Europe’s largest logistics platforms, sitting on the city’s edge. Most residents connected to these employers live in quieter districts away from the busiest leisure areas, and the security planning for their homes reflects that lower baseline rather than the city-centre picture.

item.title: Residential Security in Bilbao, Spain
item.description: Bilbao’s residential security requirements reflect the city’s position as the commercial capital of the Basque Country, with a growing executive residential market in the Abando and Indautxu districts and a distinct rural security requirement for estate properties in the surrounding Basque hills and La Rioja Alavesa wine country to the south. FCDO Spain 2024 confirms a low overall risk profile for the Bilbao area, with documented crime concentrated in the tourist-facing Casco Viejo rather than the business residential districts where executives and international assignees typically live.

item.title: Residential Security in Bridgetown
item.description: Bridgetown is the capital of Barbados and the administrative centre of one of the Caribbean’s most established offshore financial jurisdictions. The city serves as the base for a substantial international community of finance professionals, HNWI residents, and seasonal villa occupants who use the island as a primary or secondary residence. The security environment is broadly benign by Caribbean standards – the US State Dept Barbados Advisory (2024) rates the island at Level 1 (exercise normal precautions) – but this headline rating should not obscure the property-specific risks that affect waterfront villas, seasonal rentals, and marina-access properties. For a broader overview, see the Bridgetown city security profile.

item.title: Residential Security in Dammam, Saudi Arabia
item.description: Dammam and the Dhahran-Half Moon Bay residential corridor accommodate a significant international professional population, predominantly oil sector engineers, project managers, and corporate executives working for Aramco and the sector’s service company ecosystem. The FCDO Saudi Arabia travel advice (2024) records a terrorism risk against oil infrastructure and Western interests in the Eastern Province; residential security for this population addresses that specific risk alongside the standard access control and staff vetting elements applicable to any international assignee community.

item.title: Residential Security in Florence, Italy
item.description: Florence draws a substantial community of international residents, corporate assignees, and high-net-worth individuals who own or occupy properties across the city centre and the surrounding Chianti and Fiesole hills. The city’s historic building stock – narrow cortile access, shared staircases, and listed facades – creates property security requirements that differ markedly from modern residential developments. Our residential security assessments are designed for this environment, covering both the structural constraints of period properties and the specific threat profile recorded by FCDO Italy 2024 and local Questura di Firenze reporting.

item.title: Residential Security in Izmir, Turkey
item.description: Izmir’s international business community, concentrated in the Alsancak waterfront district, Karsiyaka northern shore, and Bornova university area, drives demand for residential security services oriented to the specific risk profile of Turkey’s third city. The FCDO Turkey travel advice (2024) advises enhanced precautions throughout Turkey, citing terrorism risk; the 2020 earthquake (magnitude 6.6) adds a seismic dimension that is unique to Izmir among Turkey’s major business cities. Residential security surveys in Izmir accordingly include earthquake preparedness planning alongside the standard access control and threat assessment elements.

item.title: Residential Security in Las Palmas, Spain
item.description: Las Palmas de Gran Canaria has a substantial expat residential population, drawn by the Canary Islands’ year-round climate, EU residency status, and relatively low cost of living compared to mainland Spain. The FCDO Spain travel advice (2024) records petty theft and vehicle crime in tourist and port areas as the primary security concerns; burglary and violent crime targeting residential properties is not a documented pattern at elevated rates. Residential security in Las Palmas is accordingly focused on practical deterrence, access management, and domestic staff vetting rather than counter-threat hardening.

item.title: Residential Security in Male
item.description: Male, the capital of the Maldives, is both a densely populated urban centre and the operational hub for one of the world’s most geographically dispersed luxury resort industries. Corporate assignees in the Maldives – including hospitality sector management, offshore infrastructure project staff, and financial services personnel – face a residential security environment unlike any other: many live not in the capital itself but on private resort islands accessible only by seaplane or speedboat, creating water-transport dependencies that are a defining variable in security planning. For the broader security context, see the Male city security profile.

item.title: Residential Security in Maracaibo, Venezuela
item.description: Residential security in Maracaibo operates in a fundamentally different threat environment from any European or Caribbean context. The FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Venezuela (2024), citing very high rates of violent crime, kidnapping, and home invasion. The residential security framework here starts from compound hardening and armed guard cover rather than deterrence and CCTV; the international professional population that remains in Maracaibo does so primarily to support oil sector operations, and their residential arrangements reflect a full acceptance of the high-threat baseline.

item.title: Residential Security in Medina
item.description: Medina is the second holiest city in Islam and the administrative centre of the Al-Madinah Region. Beyond its religious significance, the city hosts logistics, hospitality, and construction operations tied to the ongoing Saudi Vision 2030 expansion of the Prophet’s Mosque precinct, generating a community of contractors, project managers, and service-sector staff with residential security requirements distinct from those of Riyadh or Jeddah. For the broader city security context, see the Medina city security profile.

item.title: Residential Security in Nassau
item.description: Nassau, the capital of the Bahamas, functions simultaneously as a financial centre, a luxury real estate market, and a Caribbean gateway for HNWI clients with private aviation and yacht operations. The city’s residential security environment reflects this dual character: gated communities in the west of New Providence maintain standards comparable to secure residential estates in Florida or the Cayman Islands, while parts of central and eastern Nassau carry crime rates that the US State Dept Bahamas Advisory (2024) rates as requiring increased caution. For the broader security context, see the Nassau city security profile.

item.title: Residential Security in Nice, France
item.description: Nice and the wider Cote d’Azur present a residential security environment that differs from most other Western European cities. While FCDO France 2024 records a low overall risk level, Alpes-Maritimes police reporting identifies HNWI-targeting residential burglaries and villa intrusions as a documented regional pattern, making this one of the areas in Western Europe where a professional residential security assessment is genuinely material rather than precautionary. The concentration of high-value properties, visible wealth, and intermittently occupied villa residences creates conditions that attract targeted criminal activity, particularly during off-season periods when properties are unoccupied and monitoring is reduced.

item.title: Residential Security in Palermo, Italy
item.description: Palermo occupies a distinct position within Italy’s residential security landscape. While FCDO Italy 2024 covers Italy as a low overall risk destination, ISTAT 2022 data confirm that Palermo has above-average petty crime compared with the national average, and ACI data document vehicle and scooter theft as a specific local pattern. For HNWI principals with residential properties in Mondello, Politeama, or along the Sicilian coastline, a professional security assessment is a proportionate measure rather than an excess precaution.

item.title: Residential Security in Rotterdam, Netherlands
item.description: Rotterdam’s residential security environment reflects the city’s status as Europe’s largest port and a hub for multinational corporate activity. The substantial expat community associated with port industry, Shell, Unilever, and other major employers creates a residential market in Kop van Zuid, Kralingen, and Hillegersberg where security assessments are a routine part of property management. FCDO Netherlands 2024 guidance confirms a low overall risk profile, with documented crime in residential areas well below the levels that would place Rotterdam in a higher-risk category.

item.title: Residential Security in Seville, Spain
item.description: Seville’s residential security requirements reflect the city’s dual character: a compact, densely visited historic centre where properties sit alongside high tourist footfall, and an extensive surrounding province of Aljarafe suburban villas and Andalusian cortijo estates that present quite different access and monitoring challenges. FCDO Spain 2024 guidance positions Seville within Spain’s low overall risk profile, with documented concerns concentrated in tourist-facing public areas rather than the residential districts favoured by executives and longer-term international residents.

item.title: Residential Security in Tenerife, Spain
item.description: Tenerife has one of the largest British and northern European expat and second-home communities in the Canary Islands, with significant concentrations of HNWI residential properties along the south coast resort corridor from Costa Adeje to Los Gigantes, and a separate urban residential market in Santa Cruz de Tenerife. The island’s security environment is low-risk, with the FCDO Spain travel advice (2024) recording petty theft and vehicle crime in resort areas as the primary concerns. Residential security in Tenerife is therefore weighted toward practical deterrence, access management for properties during owner absences, and domestic staff vetting.

item.title: Residential Security in The Hague, Netherlands
item.description: The Hague occupies a unique position among European residential security environments. As the seat of the Netherlands government, the location of major international legal and disarmament bodies including the ICC, ICJ, and OPCW, and home to a substantial diplomatic community, the city presents residential security considerations that go beyond those typical of a low-crime Western European city. FCDO Netherlands 2024 confirms the Netherlands’ low overall risk profile, but AIVD 2023 reporting on espionage activity near international organisations is a specific ambient factor for residents in the Benoordenhout diplomatic quarter and the areas around the Peace Palace.

item.title: Residential Security in Thessaloniki, Greece
item.description: Thessaloniki’s corporate assignee and diplomatic community, concentrated in the Kalamaria waterfront district, Panorama suburb, and Pylaia eastern corridor, represents the primary client base for formal residential security services in the city. The FCDO Greece travel advice (2024) records occasional civil unrest in central districts and petty theft in tourist and market areas, with upmarket residential neighbourhoods at comparatively lower rates. Residential security in Thessaloniki is calibrated to this low-moderate environment: access management, domestic staff vetting, and protest-period route awareness are the core elements rather than high-level counter-threat hardening.

item.title: Residential Security in Toulouse, France
item.description: Toulouse presents a residential security landscape shaped by its twin roles as a historic southern French city and the centre of European aerospace manufacturing. The established international community near the Airbus campus at Blagnac and Colomiers creates a corporate residential market where the relevant security considerations include staff vetting against an industry context, access management for executive lets with regular short-term occupants, and an awareness that aerospace industry affiliations can elevate individual-level risk beyond the city’s generally low ambient threat profile. FCDO France 2024 guidance confirms that documented residential crime in Toulouse’s executive areas is not elevated above the national baseline.

item.title: Residential Security in Turin, Italy
item.description: Turin’s residential security environment is shaped by two distinct property types: the established apartment districts of the city, particularly Crocetta, and the wine estates and rural villas spread across the Langhe, Monferrato, and wider Piedmont countryside. Each setting presents different security considerations. City apartments require attention to shared-access infrastructure – portineria systems, condominium parking, and intercom controls – while estate properties in the wine country must address boundary definition, long approach roads, and the seasonal variation in staff and visitor numbers that comes with viticulture.

item.title: Residential Security in Verona, Italy
item.description: Verona’s residential security market is shaped by three distinct property environments: the compact historic centre, the Lake Garda villa belt to the west and north, and the Valpolicella and Soave wine estates that spread across the Veronese hills. Each presents different security considerations. Historic Citta Antica apartments require access solutions that respect listed-building constraints while addressing the shared-entry vulnerabilities typical of divided palazzi. Lake Garda villas must account for lakeside frontage, boat-dock access, and the seasonal road congestion that can significantly affect emergency response times on the lake road network during peak summer. FCDO Italy 2024 guidance confirms a low overall risk profile across all three zones.

item.title: Residential Security in Astana
item.description: Astana, Kazakhstan’s planned capital on the northern steppe, houses the headquarters of sovereign wealth fund Samruk-Kazyna, the energy sector ministries overseeing the Tengiz and Kashagan fields, and a growing diplomatic community. The city’s security environment is broadly stable, but the January 2022 nationwide protests demonstrated that civil disorder can reach the capital quickly, and the energy sector’s profile attracts counter-intelligence interest documented by the US State Dept. For the Astana city security briefing covering current threat levels and transit considerations, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Barranquilla
item.description:
Barranquilla residential security addresses a high-crime environment where the city’s established expat and executive residential districts – El Prado, Altos del Prado, and Zona Norte – provide a better baseline than most of the metropolitan area but are not insulated from the broader security environment of Atlantico department.

The SVSP regulatory framework governs all commercial residential security in Colombia. All operators hold current SVSP licences under Decreto 356/1994. The Barranquilla city briefing provides the full advisory context for Atlantico. For personal security cover during Barranquilla movements, close protection officers in Barranquilla covers the SVSP-licensed protective arrangement.

item.title: Residential Security in Basra
item.description: Basra is Iraq’s principal oil and gas hub, hosting operations for BP (Rumaila), ExxonMobil (West Qurna), TotalEnergies, and Lukoil, among others. The security environment is high-risk: FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Basra Governorate, and the US State Dept rates Iraq at Level 4: Do Not Travel. FCDO specifically flags kidnapping risk for foreign nationals and documents mortar and rocket attacks on energy infrastructure and compound areas. The 2018 protests that targeted energy company offices and compounds in Basra demonstrated that periods of civil unrest can escalate to direct attacks on energy-sector infrastructure. For the full Basra city security briefing, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Bern
item.description: Bern is Switzerland’s federal capital and the seat of the country’s diplomatic community, intergovernmental offices, and federal government institutions. The security environment is among the lowest-risk in Europe for residential purposes, with excellent emergency services and well-maintained public spaces. However, the concentration of diplomatic, pharmaceutical, and financial services personnel in the city makes it a target for foreign state-sponsored espionage, a risk explicitly noted by Switzerland’s NDB in its Annual Report 2023. For a broader overview of security in the city, see our Bern city security briefing.

item.title: Residential Security in Cali
item.description:
Residential security for Cali assignees addresses a high risk environment shaped by Gang crime, carjacking, kidnapping, and extortion by criminal organisations. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through SuperVigilancia (SVSP - Superintendencia de Vigilancia y Seguridad Privada)-compliant operators.

The Cali city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Cali covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Bogota.

item.title: Residential Security in Cebu
item.description:
Cebu residential security addresses a moderate-risk environment in the Philippines’ second-largest urban centre, where the IT and BPO sector-driven expat community is concentrated in the high-rise developments of Cebu Business Park and IT Park. The security priorities are condominium building security quality, domestic staff vetting, and typhoon emergency preparedness – rather than the intensive programmes warranted in higher-risk Philippine cities.

All operators in Cebu hold current PNP-PSG licences under Republic Act 5487. The Cebu city briefing provides the full advisory context for Central Visayas. For personal security cover during Cebu business visits, executive protection in Cebu covers the PNP-supervised close protection arrangement.

item.title: Residential Security in Chengdu
item.description:
Chengdu residential security operates in one of China’s most liveable major cities, with a lower ambient crime profile than coastal megacities. Physical security threats at residential properties are low by Chinese standards. The relevant security considerations are the digital and legal environment common to all Chinese cities: domestic staff vetting, communications security for principals in sensitive sectors, and legal risk awareness around the exit ban framework.

Chengdu’s seismic environment adds an emergency preparedness dimension not present in most other markets. All security arrangements are delivered through MPS-compliant structures per the Security Services Management Regulations. The Chengdu city briefing provides the full FCDO advisory context. For principals also requiring executive protection during Chengdu business visits, executive protection in Chengdu covers the PSB-compliant close protection arrangement.

item.title: Residential Security in Durban
item.description: Durban residential security is shaped by KwaZulu-Natal’s well-documented crime environment and the practical challenges of load shedding continuity. SAPS KwaZulu-Natal Crime Stats 2024 records home invasion and armed robbery as among the most reported serious crimes in the eThekwini metropolitan area; the question for most expat households is whether their security infrastructure – particularly armed response, electric fencing, and CCTV – is genuinely fit for the specific property and area, not merely nominally in place.

item.title: Residential Security in Fortaleza
item.description:
Residential security for Fortaleza assignees addresses a high risk environment shaped by Gang violence (Ceara has one of Brazil’s highest homicide rates), armed robbery, and carjacking. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through SSPDS-CE (Secretaria da Seguranca Publica e Defesa Social do Ceara)-compliant operators.

The Fortaleza city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Fortaleza covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Recife.

item.title: Residential Security in Guangzhou
item.description:
Guangzhou residential security addresses the specific profile of China’s primary trade and manufacturing hub, where the international assignee population is dominated by procurement, product development, and supply chain professionals. Physical crime in Tianhe and Zhujiang New Town is moderate; the security priorities are digital communications security during trade negotiations, domestic staff vetting, and legal risk awareness around China’s exit ban framework during commercial disputes.

All security arrangements are delivered through MPS-compliant structures per the Security Services Management Regulations. The Guangzhou city briefing provides the full FCDO advisory context and trade-cycle security considerations. For principals requiring executive protection, executive protection in Guangzhou covers the compliant close protection arrangement for Canton Fair and corporate visits.

item.title: Residential Security in Havana
item.description: Havana presents one of the most distinctive residential security environments in our network. Cuba’s socialist governance structure means that conventional private security provision does not exist in the form available elsewhere; the absence of independent operators is a structural given, not a gap to be filled with informal alternatives. The risks that do exist are primarily concentrated in the communications and medical domains: FCDO Cuba travel advice (2024) notes MININT monitoring of foreign nationals’ communications and highlights medical facility supply constraints. Petty crime targeting foreign nationals (jinetero approaches, distraction theft in Habana Vieja and Centro Habana) is a secondary but documented concern. For the full Havana city security briefing covering transit and daily movement considerations, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Ibadan
item.description: Ibadan is one of Nigeria’s largest cities and the capital of Oyo State, with a substantial university sector (University of Ibadan, founded 1948, is Nigeria’s oldest university), government functions, and manufacturing base. The security environment is high: FCDO Nigeria travel advice (2024) notes armed robbery risk throughout Nigeria with night-time movement particularly high-risk, and flags kidnapping risk for foreign nationals. Communal violence between Fulani and Yoruba communities has been documented in peri-urban Ibadan. For the full Ibadan city security briefing covering transit and movement risk, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Krakow
item.description: Krakow has developed into one of Central Europe’s leading technology and business-process-outsourcing hubs, attracting a growing population of corporate assignees from across Europe, the US, and Asia. The city’s overall security environment is low, with crime rates below the Western European average and an effective police service. The risks that do exist for residential assignees are concentrated in tourist areas rather than the suburban and professional zones where most corporate staff are placed. For the full Krakow city security briefing, including transit and event considerations, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Kumasi
item.description: Kumasi is Ghana’s second city and the commercial capital of the Ashanti Region, with an economy built primarily around gold mining, timber, and cocoa, alongside a growing commercial services sector. The security environment is moderate: Ghana is one of West Africa’s more stable democracies, but Kumasi carries petty-crime, road-safety, and communal-tension risks that require active residential security management. For the full Kumasi city security briefing covering transit and movement considerations, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Las Vegas
item.description: Las Vegas is best known as an entertainment destination, but it hosts a substantial corporate population across conventions and events, entertainment-sector regional offices, and hospitality-industry management. The Las Vegas Valley’s security environment is moderate overall, with sharp geographic variation: Summerlin and Henderson maintain crime profiles broadly comparable to upmarket suburban areas in other US cities, while Strip-adjacent and Fremont Street zones carry significantly higher robbery and violent crime rates per LVMPD data. For the full Las Vegas city security briefing covering transit risk and event-attendance considerations, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Lilongwe
item.description:
Residential security for Lilongwe assignees addresses a moderate risk environment shaped by Opportunistic crime, vehicle crime, and road safety risks. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through Malawi Police Service licensing under the Security Services Act-compliant operators.

The Lilongwe city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Lilongwe covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Lusaka.

item.title: Residential Security in Macao
item.description:
Residential security for Macao assignees addresses a low risk environment shaped by Very low street crime; primary security considerations are gaming sector risk (VIP client protection, high-value asset security), and Macao’s position within China’s national security law jurisdiction. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through Security Police Force (PSP - Policia de Seguranca Publica) under Law No. 2/2002-compliant operators.

The Macao city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Macao covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Hong Kong.

item.title: Residential Security in Mandalay
item.description:
Residential security for Mandalay assignees addresses a critical risk environment shaped by Armed conflict (post-2021 coup civil war), risk of arbitrary detention, and infrastructure disruption. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through Myanmar Police Force (post-2021 coup framework)-compliant operators.

The Mandalay city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Mandalay covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Bangkok.

item.title: Residential Security in Marseille
item.description: Marseille is France’s second city and its principal maritime hub, housing major port operations, petrochemical facilities at Fos-sur-Mer, and a growing pharmaceutical sector. The security environment in Marseille is more varied than in any other major French city: the 7e and 8e arrondissements and the Aix-en-Provence commuter belt offer a broadly appropriate residential environment for corporate assignees, while the northern arrondissements carry an organised crime risk level unacceptable for unprotected foreign national residential placement. French Interior Ministry statistics identify Marseille as having the highest organised crime concentration of any French city. For the full Marseille city security briefing covering transit and event considerations, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Monterrey
item.description:
Monterrey residential security is not a peripheral concern for senior executive households in the metropolitan area. Nuevo Leon’s security environment – shaped by historical cartel activity and documented kidnapping risk – means that professional residential security assessment and planning is the standard baseline for multinational executives, not a luxury measure.

The physical and legal security combination in Monterrey requires attention to both property hardening and the human element: domestic staff vetting, routine variation, and safe room capability are the three most consistently identified gaps in assessments of expat residential properties in San Pedro Garza Garcia. For related protective services, see the Monterrey city briefing and close protection officers in Monterrey for personal security cover during ground movements.

item.title: Residential Security in Nanjing
item.description:
Residential security for Nanjing assignees addresses a low risk environment shaped by National security law exposure, digital monitoring environment, and commercial intelligence sensitivity in Nanjing’s advanced manufacturing and semiconductor sectors. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through Ministry of Public Security (MPS) under MPS Order No. 564 (2010), administered locally by the Nanjing Public Security Bureau-compliant operators.

The Nanjing city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Nanjing covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Shanghai.

item.title: Residential Security in Port Moresby
item.description:
Residential security for Port Moresby assignees addresses a high risk environment shaped by Raskol gang activity, carjacking, and kidnap risk. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through Security (Protection) Act 2004, administered by the PNG Police Force-compliant operators.

The Port Moresby city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Port Moresby covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Brisbane.

item.title: Residential Security in Porto Alegre
item.description:
Porto Alegre residential security operates in Rio Grande do Sul’s highest-crime major city by SESP-RS 2024 metrics. Moinhos de Vento and the city’s other high-income residential districts have meaningfully better security infrastructure than peripheral areas, but they are not immune from the crime environment that makes professional residential security planning the appropriate baseline for senior expat and HNWI households.

The May 2024 floods added a post-disaster infrastructure dimension to residential assessments for lower-elevation properties. All operators hold current Policia Federal licences per Portaria 387/2006. The Porto Alegre city briefing provides the full advisory context for Rio Grande do Sul. For personal security cover during Porto Alegre movements, close protection officers in Porto Alegre covers the licensed close protection arrangement.

item.title: Residential Security in Pune
item.description: Pune residential security addresses a moderate-risk environment where the established expat districts of Koregaon Park, Kalyani Nagar, and Viman Nagar offer a sound baseline of gated society security infrastructure. The relevant security priorities are the quality of that gated society infrastructure (which varies significantly between developments), domestic staff vetting, and basic property access control – rather than the more intensive programmes warranted in higher-risk South African or Latin American cities.

item.title: Residential Security in Recife
item.description:
Residential security for Recife assignees addresses a high risk environment shaped by Armed robbery, express kidnapping, carjacking, and gang violence. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through SESP-PE (Secretaria de Seguranca Publica e Defesa Social de Pernambuco)-compliant operators.

The Recife city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Recife covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Sao Paulo.

item.title: Residential Security in Sharm el-Sheikh
item.description: Residential security for Sharm el-Sheikh assignees addresses a critical risk environment shaped by FCDO advises against all but essential travel to the whole of the Sinai Peninsula (except the Sharm el-Sheikh resort area); terrorist and extremist attack risk based on the 2015 Metrojet bombing and historical Naama Bay incidents. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through Egyptian Ministry of Interior under Law No. 116 of 2008-compliant operators.

item.title: Residential Security in Shenzhen
item.description:
Shenzhen residential security for corporate assignees addresses a risk profile shaped more by the digital and legal environment than by street crime. The Shekou and Futian expat residential districts have a manageable physical crime profile; the security considerations that matter most are domestic staff vetting in a technology-intelligence environment, digital communications security in a state-monitored infrastructure, and legal risk awareness for principals with significant commercial activity.

All security arrangements for Shenzhen are delivered through MPS-compliant structures per China’s Security Services Management Regulations (MPS Order No. 564, 2010). The Shenzhen city briefing provides the full FCDO advisory context on commercial risk, digital monitoring, and emergency procedures. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Shenzhen covers the compliant close protection arrangement.

item.title: Residential Security in Surabaya
item.description:
Surabaya residential security addresses a moderate-risk environment in East Java’s largest city, where the established expat residential districts of Pakuwon City and Ciputra World provide a reasonable baseline of compound security infrastructure. The security priorities are compound quality assessment, domestic staff vetting, and seasonal emergency preparedness for flooding – rather than the intensive residential security programmes warranted in higher-risk Indonesian cities.

All operators engaged for Surabaya residential assignments hold current POLRI licences under Police Regulation No. 4/2020. The Surabaya city briefing provides the full advisory context for East Java. For personal security cover during Surabaya business visits, executive protection in Surabaya covers the POLRI-licensed close protection arrangement.

item.title: Residential Security in Valencia
item.description: Valencia is Spain’s third city and an established base for automotive, port-logistics, and tourism-sector corporate operations. The city’s security environment is low overall, with a Mediterranean quality of life and effective public services. The risks that exist for residential assignees are primarily concentrated in tourist and beach-district zones, where seasonal footfall creates petty-crime exposure, and in the terrorism-awareness framework maintained nationally since 2017. For the full Valencia city security briefing covering transit and event security considerations, see our dedicated city page.

item.title: Residential Security in Wuhan
item.description:
Residential security for Wuhan assignees addresses a low-moderate risk environment shaped by Business intelligence sensitivity in automotive and manufacturing sectors, national security law exposure, digital monitoring environment. Property assessments, domestic staff vetting, and emergency protocols are delivered through Ministry of Public Security (MPS) under MPS Order No. 564 (2010), administered locally by the Wuhan Public Security Bureau-compliant operators.

The Wuhan city briefing provides the full advisory context and threat breakdown. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Wuhan covers the compliant close protection arrangement. For regional context, see residential security in Shanghai.

item.title: Residential Security in Adelaide
item.description: Residential security in Adelaide addresses the specific threat context identified in local police crime data: low. SAPOL Crime Statistics 2024 record low overall crime in the CBD and North Adelaide; Hindley Street after 22:00 on weekends is the primary elevated-risk zone. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in North Adelaide period residences, Stamford Grand Glenelg, and InterContinental Adelaide suites, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under Security and Investigation Industry Act 1995 (SA), administered by Consumer and Business Services (CBS) SA and vetted to a documented standard.

item.title: Residential Security in Atlanta
item.description:
Residential security in Atlanta addresses the specific threat context identified in the APD Crime Report 2025: carjacking citywide. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Buckhead residential enclave and Sandy Springs gated communities, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under OCGA Title 43, Ch 38 and vetted to a documented standard.

Property assessment as the foundation

A written property security assessment is the non-negotiable starting point for residential security in Atlanta. It identifies the specific vulnerabilities at the subject property, benchmarks them against the local threat picture, and produces a prioritised remediation schedule. A security provider that recommends a guarding contract without a prior written assessment has inverted the correct sequence.

item.title: Residential Security in Austin
item.description: Residential security in Austin addresses the specific threat context identified in local police crime data: moderate. APD Annual Report 2024 records elevated property crime and vehicle theft, concentrated in outer East Austin and some Downtown parking structures. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Domain North, Barton Hills, and Westlake luxury accommodation, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under Texas Occupations Code, Chapter 1702 (Texas DPS Private Security Bureau) and vetted to a documented standard.

item.title: Residential Security in Boston
item.description:
Residential security in Boston addresses the specific threat context identified in the BPD Crime Report 2025: very low. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Back Bay brownstones and Cambridge residential estates, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under MGL c.147, ss.22-30 and vetted to a documented standard.

Property assessment as the foundation

A written property security assessment is the non-negotiable starting point for residential security in Boston. It identifies the specific vulnerabilities at the subject property, benchmarks them against the local threat picture, and produces a prioritised remediation schedule. A security provider that recommends a guarding contract without a prior written assessment has inverted the correct sequence.

item.title: Residential Security in Brisbane
item.description: Residential security in Brisbane addresses the specific threat context identified in local police crime data: low. QPS Crime Statistics 2024 records low violent crime in the CBD and South Bank; opportunistic property crime and alcohol-related disorder occur in Fortitude Valley after dark. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in New Farm and Ascot, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under Security Providers Act 1993 (Queensland Police Service Weapons Licensing Branch) and vetted to a documented standard.

item.title: Residential Security in Busan
item.description: Residential security in Busan addresses the specific threat context identified in local police crime data: low. NPA Crime Statistics 2024 records low violent crime across the city; minor disorder in older port-adjacent entertainment areas after midnight; persistent geopolitical background risk from North Korea factored into contingency planning. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Haeundae Marine City and Centum City, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under Security Services Industry Act (경비업법), National Police Agency of Korea and vetted to a documented standard.

item.title: Residential Security in Calgary
item.description: Residential security in Calgary addresses the specific threat context identified in local police crime data: low. CPS Crime Statistics 2024 records low violent crime in the Downtown Core; elevated property crime citywide; severe winter conditions October through March require adapted vehicle protocols. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Mount Royal and Elbow Park, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under Security Services and Investigators Act (Alberta Justice and Solicitor General) and vetted to a documented standard.

item.title: Residential Security in Chennai
item.description: Residential security in Chennai addresses the specific threat context identified in local police crime data: moderate. Tamil Nadu Police Crime Statistics 2024 record elevated road traffic incidents and petty theft in crowded market areas; monsoon season from October to December introduces additional movement disruption. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Adyar upscale residences, Boat Club Road HNWI accommodation, and Leela Palace suites, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under PSARA 2005, Tamil Nadu Police Private Security Agency Licensing Authority and vetted to a documented standard.

item.title: Residential Security in Chicago
item.description:
Residential security in Chicago addresses the specific threat context identified in the CPD Crime Report 2025: carjacking elevated citywide. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Gold Coast and Lincoln Park HNWI residential zones, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under Illinois PSISA 2004 (IDFPR) and vetted to a documented standard.

Property assessment as the foundation

A written property security assessment is the non-negotiable starting point for residential security in Chicago. item.title: Residential Security in Dallas
item.description: Residential security in Dallas addresses the specific threat context identified in local police crime data: moderate. DPD Crime Report 2024 records elevated property crime and carjacking across the wider metroplex, concentrated away from the Downtown core and Uptown. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Highland Park and University Park, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. All guarding personnel are licensed under Texas Occupations Code, Chapter 1702 (Texas DPS Private Security Bureau) and vetted to a documented standard.

item.title: Residential Security in Denver
item.description: Residential security in Denver addresses the specific threat context identified in local police crime data: moderate. DPD Crime Report 2024 records elevated property crime and drug-related disorder in specific zones; altitude (1,609m) is a medical planning consideration for newly arrived international principals. For HNWI principals and senior executives with residential properties in Cherry Creek and Greenwood Village, a structured residential security programme provides measurably more protection than ad hoc or uncoordinated measures. item.title: Residential Security in Barcelona, Spain
item.description:

A professional residential security deployment for Barcelona begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk across Eixample and Diagonal business corridor; Sarrià-Sant Gervasi for residential. Normal precautions for estonia (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Estonia (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Tallinn begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Police and Border Guard Board (PPA). Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility North Estonia Medical Centre (PERH, medevac to Helsinki or Stockholm), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.description:
Residential security in Antananarivo is structured around registered Malagasy operators, the Isoraka district as the established expatriate residential zone, and comprehensive staff vetting adapted to the Malagasy market. Mining sector companies, development finance organisations, and NGOs with Antananarivo residential programmes operate against a medium-risk security backdrop of violent street crime, carjacking, and periodic political instability. Source: FCDO Madagascar travel advice (2026); US State Dept Madagascar Level 2 advisory (2026).

For the full Antananarivo security picture, see our Antananarivo city briefing. Clients requiring personal close protection alongside residential security can review executive protection in Antananarivo.

item.description:

Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; transnistria against all travel; us state dept level 2 (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Chisinau begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Moldovan Ministry of Internal Affairs. Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility Spitalul Clinic Republican, medevac to Bucharest), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.description:

A professional residential security deployment for Cotonou begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Beninese Ministry of Interior and Public Security.
