item.title: Residential Security in Freetown
item.description: Residential security in Freetown operates in a high-risk environment requiring comprehensive compound hardening, Private Security Industry Act-licensed guard teams, Aberdeen peninsula or Hill Station as the recommended residential zones, and a West Africa medevac capability pre-positioned before any programme commences. Post-election political unrest adds political monitoring and evacuation planning to the standard residential security requirements. Source: FCDO Sierra Leone travel advice (2026); US State Dept Sierra Leone Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Gaborone
item.description:
Residential security in Gaborone operates within Botswana’s low-risk, well-regulated environment. Diamond sector expatriates, development finance professionals, and diplomatic staff benefit from Private Security Industry Regulation Act-licensed operators, proportionate compound hardening, and domestic staff vetting that takes advantage of Botswana’s functional civil registry and police clearance system. Source: FCDO Botswana travel advice (2026).

For the full Gaborone security context, see our Gaborone city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Gaborone.

item.description:

A professional residential security deployment for Lome begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. A professional residential security deployment for Malabo begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. item.title: Residential Security in Maseru
item.description:
Residential security in Maseru is structured around LMPS-licensed operators, the Maseru Central diplomatic area as the recommended residential zone, and medium-risk hardening specifications that address Lesotho’s documented armed robbery and vehicle crime environment. Diamond sector and development finance expatriates benefit from South Africa’s proximity for both emergency evacuation and medical medevac routing. Source: FCDO Lesotho travel advice (2026).

For the full Maseru security context, see our Maseru city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Maseru.

item.title: Residential Security in Mbabane
item.description:
Residential security in Mbabane operates within Eswatini’s low-risk, small-market operating environment. Agribusiness, manufacturing, and diplomatic residential programmes benefit from licensed operators with Eswatini-specific credentials, the Ezulwini Valley as the primary long-term residential zone, and medevac planning via South Africa that takes advantage of the country’s proximity to Nelspruit and Johannesburg. Source: FCDO Eswatini travel advice (2026).

For the full Mbabane security context, see our Mbabane city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Mbabane.

item.title: Residential Security in Monrovia
item.description: Residential security in Monrovia operates in a high-risk post-conflict environment requiring comprehensive compound hardening, LNP-licensed guard teams, Mamba Point or Sinkor as the recommended residential zones, and West Africa medevac capability pre-positioned before any programme commences. The post-conflict institutional fragility that limits police response capacity drives a higher compound hardening and guard staffing standard than in comparable-risk capitals. Source: FCDO Liberia travel advice (2026); US State Dept Liberia Level 2 advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Ouagadougou
item.description: Residential security in Ouagadougou is not a viable programme under the current extreme risk conditions. The FCDO advises against all travel to Burkina Faso, including Ouagadougou, and the US State Department classifies the country at Level 4 (Do Not Travel). This page serves organisations monitoring the situation, managing wind-down of prior programmes, or planning remote management from Abidjan or Accra. Source: FCDO Burkina Faso travel advice (2026); US State Dept Burkina Faso Level 4 advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Podgorica, Montenegro
item.description:
Residential security in Podgorica addresses the specific requirements of expatriate executives, diplomatic staff, and high-net-worth individuals requiring protected accommodation in Montenegrin organised crime and inter-clan violence environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution, specifically noting bystander risk from criminal violence; us state dept level 2 (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Podgorica begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Montenegrin Ministry of Interior. Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility Clinical Center of Montenegro, medevac to Belgrade or Vienna), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.title: Residential Security in Port Louis
item.description:
Residential security in Port Louis operates within Mauritius’s low-risk environment and well-regulated PSIA framework. Financial sector expatriates, family office principals, and HNWI residents benefit from PSIA-licensed residential guard teams, proportionate access management, and programme elements calibrated for a low-threat, high-privacy operating environment. Cyclone season planning is the most materially distinct element of Mauritius residential programmes compared with continental African deployments. Source: FCDO Mauritius travel advice (2026).

For the full Port Louis security context, see our Port Louis city briefing. Clients requiring personal security alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Port Louis.

item.title: Residential Security in Praia, Cape Verde
item.description:

A professional residential security deployment for Praia begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. item.description:

Fcdo advises a high degree of caution and warns of landmine risk outside urban areas; us state dept level 2 (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Sarajevo begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Federation of BiH Ministry of Interior (cantonal level). Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility University Clinical Centre Sarajevo, medevac to Vienna or Zagreb), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

A professional residential security deployment for Skopje begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Ministry of Interior of North Macedonia. Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility General Hospital Skopje (8th September), medevac to Belgrade or Vienna), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.description:

Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; us state dept level 2 (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Tirana begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. item.title: Residential Security in Victoria, Seychelles
item.description:
Residential security in Victoria, Seychelles is calibrated for the low-risk Indian Ocean island environment, focusing on personal privacy, access management, domestic staff vetting, and outer island transfer security for HNWI and financial sector residents. The compact Mahe geography and the island’s international community create a distinct operating context from continental African residential programmes. Source: FCDO Seychelles travel advice (2026).

For the full Victoria security context, see our Victoria, Seychelles city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Victoria.

item.title: Residential Security in Windhoek
item.description:
Residential security in Windhoek operates within Namibia’s low-risk, regulated private security environment. Mining sector expatriates, corporate residential staff, and diplomatic personnel benefit from Security Officers’ Act-licensed operators, Klein Windhoek as the established residential zone, and vehicle security planning that addresses the specific break-in risk in commercial areas. Source: FCDO Namibia travel advice (2026).

For the full Windhoek security context, see our Windhoek city briefing. Clients requiring personal EP alongside their residential programme can review executive protection in Windhoek.

item.title: Residential Security in Yerevan, Armenia
item.description:
Residential security in Yerevan addresses the specific requirements of expatriate executives, diplomatic staff, and high-net-worth individuals requiring protected accommodation in Armenian post-conflict geopolitical environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; us state dept level 2, border areas level 3 (2026).

A professional residential security deployment for Yerevan begins with a property security survey covering perimeter, access control, natural surveillance, lighting, and neighbourhood risk. Guard-force selection follows, with all personnel vetted to the standards of the Armenian Police. Emergency response planning – covering medical emergency (nearest facility Nairi Medical Centre, medevac to Tbilisi or Istanbul), fire, intrusion, and civil disturbance – is completed before occupancy begins.

item.title: Residential Security in Asmara
item.description: Residential security in Asmara operates within constraints that have no parallel in other African capitals: no commercial private security sector, systematic government surveillance of foreign nationals, exit visa requirements, and the structural realities of one of Africa’s most closed authoritarian states. The US State Department rates Eritrea at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO advises a high degree of caution. The international resident population in Asmara is very small: diplomatic mission staff, mining sector employees connected with the Bisha mine, and a limited UN and NGO presence.

item.title: Residential Security in Bangui
item.description: Residential security in Bangui operates within the parameters of an active armed conflict rather than as a standard expatriate residential security programme. Protected compound structures – UN compounds, ICRC facilities, diplomatic compounds – are the residential baseline for all personnel in Bangui. FCDO advises against all travel to the CAR, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 4 (Do Not Travel) as of 2026. Conflict-zone compound security standards, MINUSCA coordination, and mandatory medevac planning are the structural elements of any Bangui residential programme.

item.title: Residential Security in Brazzaville
item.description: Residential security in Brazzaville is structured around ministerial-licensed operators, the Plateau district as the recommended residential zone, and systematic staff vetting in a high-risk operating environment shaped by the Republic of Congo’s recent political violence history and persistent street crime. Oil sector companies (ENI, TotalEnergies) and development finance and NGO organisations with residential programmes in Brazzaville maintain licensed compound guarding, generator-backed access control and CCTV, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response planning as the standard residential security baseline. Source: FCDO Republic of Congo travel advice (2026); Ministry of Interior Republic of Congo.

item.title: Residential Security in Bujumbura
item.description: Residential security in Bujumbura is structured around Ministry of Interior-licensed operators with SNR-vetted credentials, the Rohero and Quartier Asiatique districts as the established residential zones, and systematic staff vetting adapted to Burundi’s intercommunal history. Development finance organisations, NGOs, and commercial sector companies with Bujumbura residential programmes operate against a backdrop shaped by the 2015 political crisis and its aftermath: current political monitoring, updated evacuation plans, and hardened compound specifications are standard elements of any Bujumbura residential security programme. Source: FCDO Burundi travel advice (2026); Ministry of Interior Burundi.

item.title: Residential Security in Djibouti City
item.description: Residential security in Djibouti City operates in the most manageable security environment of the Horn of Africa region, shaped by the presence of multiple international military bases, an internationally connected port and logistics sector, and a government that has maintained relative political stability. DST-licensed operators provide the regulatory framework; the Plateau du Serpent and Heron area are the established residential zones for international business and military logistics personnel. High-temperature equipment specification and French-Arabic language capability in the guard force are specific Djibouti City residential requirements. Source: FCDO Djibouti travel advice (2026); DST Djibouti.

item.title: Residential Security in Hargeisa
item.description: Residential security in Hargeisa operates in the most stable part of the Somali territories. The Somaliland government maintains effective administrative and security control of the capital, and a well-established international community of development finance organisations, NGOs, and trade personnel has accumulated compound security infrastructure over many years. Somaliland Ministry of Interior-licensed operators provide the guard force framework; clan dynamics require operator-specific local knowledge for domestic staff vetting. FCDO travel advice (2026) notes Hargeisa’s differentiated risk from southern Somalia while advising a high degree of caution. Source: FCDO Somalia (Somaliland) travel advice (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Juba
item.description: Residential security in Juba applies conflict-history standards shaped by the city’s 2016 urban fighting: reinforced compound perimeters, multi-layered access control, armed guard capability, hardened shelters, CCTV with generator backup, and UNMISS coordination as a life-safety element. The US State Department rates South Sudan at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO advises a high degree of caution. Oil sector companies, UN agencies, and NGOs with Juba residential programmes operate within this framework, with Hai Malakal and Hai Amarat as the established expatriate residential districts. Source: FCDO South Sudan travel advice (2026); UNMISS operational reporting (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Khartoum
item.description: Residential security in Khartoum is effectively suspended under the conditions of Sudan’s active civil war between the SAF and RSF, which has been ongoing since April 2023. Khartoum’s residential infrastructure has been severely damaged, expatriate programmes have been evacuated, and no standard commercial residential security service operates in the city under current conditions. FCDO advises against all travel to Sudan, and the US State Department rates Sudan at Level 4 (Do Not Travel) as of 2026.

item.title: Residential Security in Libreville
item.description: Residential security in Libreville is structured around Ministry of Interior-licensed operators, the Centre Ville and Boulevard Triomphal corridor as the established expatriate residential zones, and systematic staff vetting in an environment affected by Gabon’s August 2023 military transition. Oil sector companies (TotalEnergies, Perenco) and mining operations (Comilog, Eramet) with residential programmes in Libreville maintain licensed compound guarding, access control, CCTV, and domestic staff vetting as the standard residential security baseline. The transition has increased the importance of current emergency response planning and confirmed evacuation routing. Source: FCDO Gabon travel advice (2026); Ministry of Interior Gabon.

item.title: Residential Security in N'Djamena
item.description: Residential security in N’Djamena is structured around Ministry of Public Security-licensed operators, the Bololo district as the recommended residential zone, and systematic staff vetting in a high-risk operating environment shaped by Chad’s transitional military governance, Sahel regional context, and high-risk border environment. Oil sector companies (ExxonMobil, CNPC, TotalEnergies) and major humanitarian organisations (UNHCR, WFP, ICRC) with residential programmes in N’Djamena maintain licensed compound guarding, generator-backed access control and CCTV, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response and medevac planning as the residential security baseline. Source: FCDO Chad travel advice (2026); Ministry of Public Security Chad.

item.title: Residential Security in Ashgabat
item.description:
Residential security in Ashgabat addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Ashgabat

Expatriate residential security in Ashgabat addresses heavy state surveillance, movement restrictions outside Ashgabat, and the regulatory constraints of operating in a highly controlled authoritarian environment and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Turkmenistan travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Ashgabat

A residential security programme in Ashgabat covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Asuncion
item.description:
Residential security in Asuncion addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Asuncion

Expatriate residential security in Asuncion addresses petty crime, bag-snatching, and express kidnapping risk in the informal economy districts and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Paraguay travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Asuncion

A residential security programme in Asuncion covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Belize City
item.description: Residential security in Belize City is structured around a high crime rate, limited emergency response capacity, and the additional physical risk of Belize’s hurricane season. The recommended residential zones in Buttonwood Bay and Belama provide the most appropriate base for international personnel, separated from the gang-controlled areas of central Belize City’s Southside district. Generator backup and storm-season preparation are programme essentials rather than optional enhancements. Source: FCDO Belize travel advice (2026); US State Dept Belize Level 2 travel advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Bishkek
item.description:
Residential security in Bishkek addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Bishkek

Expatriate residential security in Bishkek addresses petty crime, periodic political unrest, and organised criminal networks with documented involvement in cargo theft and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Kyrgyzstan travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Bishkek

A residential security programme in Bishkek covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Chittagong
item.description:
Residential security in Chittagong addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Chittagong

Expatriate residential security in Chittagong addresses terrorism, political strikes (hartals), and petty crime targeting port and garment-sector visitors and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Bangladesh travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Chittagong

A residential security programme in Chittagong covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Dushanbe
item.description:
Residential security in Dushanbe addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Dushanbe

Expatriate residential security in Dushanbe addresses border proximity risks, political sensitivity, and petty crime in market areas and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Tajikistan travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Dushanbe

A residential security programme in Dushanbe covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Georgetown
item.description: Residential security in Georgetown has become a priority concern as Guyana’s oil sector expansion has driven a rapid influx of international personnel and increased the visibility of expatriate residential properties. Georgetown’s crime rate is elevated, with armed robbery and home invasion incidents documented in expatriate-occupied areas. The Guyana Police Force has limited capacity relative to the pace of the city’s growth, and private residential security programmes are the primary mitigation for international staff based in the city. Source: FCDO Guyana travel advice (2026); US State Dept Guyana Level 3 travel advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Guatemala City
item.description: Residential security in Guatemala City requires structured compound protection, systematic staff vetting, and explicit anti-kidnapping protocols for any international executive or family based in the city. Guatemala City has a high crime rate with kidnapping, extortion, and violent crime affecting multiple zones. The recommended residential zones in Zone 10, Zone 14, and Zone 15 (Vista Hermosa) provide the most appropriate security environment, but structured residential security programming is the required baseline rather than an optional enhancement. Source: FCDO Guatemala travel advice (2026); US State Dept Guatemala Level 3 travel advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Guayaquil
item.description:
Residential security in Guayaquil addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Guayaquil

Expatriate residential security in Guayaquil addresses armed robbery, kidnapping for ransom, carjacking, and gang-related violence linked to drug trafficking networks with significant presence in the port and peripheral neighbourhoods and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Ecuador travel advice (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Kingston
item.description: Residential security in Kingston is a response to a genuinely elevated threat environment. Jamaica’s murder rate of approximately 44 homicides per 100,000 population in 2023 (Jamaica Constabulary Force statistics) is among the highest in the Western Hemisphere, driven by gang activity in defined communities of West and Downtown Kingston. The UK FCDO Jamaica travel advice (2026) advises against all travel to a specific list of Kingston communities. Expatriates and international staff based in Kingston require a structured residential programme that addresses compound security, staff vetting, and emergency response from first arrival.

item.title: Residential Security in La Paz
item.description:
Residential security in La Paz addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in La Paz

Expatriate residential security in La Paz addresses street crime, express kidnapping, political demonstrations that can block major routes, and altitude-related medical risk at 3,640m above sea level and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Bolivia travel advice (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Managua
item.description: Residential security in Managua addresses both conventional crime risks and the specific challenges created by Nicaragua’s political environment under the Ortega administration. The combination of a conventional high-crime urban environment and the political risk of surveillance, arbitrary detention, and suppression of independent civil society makes residential security planning in Managua more complex than in most Central American capitals. The recommended residential zones in Carretera Masaya, Altamira, and Las Colinas provide the most appropriate base for international staff. Source: FCDO Nicaragua travel advice (2026); US State Dept Nicaragua Level 3 travel advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Montevideo
item.description:
Residential security in Montevideo addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Montevideo

Expatriate residential security in Montevideo addresses petty theft in tourist areas and peripheral neighbourhood crime, both at low levels relative to Latin American regional norms and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Uruguay travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Montevideo

A residential security programme in Montevideo covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Paramaribo
item.description: Residential security in Paramaribo is calibrated to the city’s medium risk profile: a structured programme addressing property crime, access control, and emergency response planning, without the full-scale compound guarding required in Kingston or Port-au-Prince. Paramaribo is a relatively stable Caribbean capital, but petty crime and opportunistic burglary affect residential properties where perimeter security is absent. The city’s mining and energy sector has brought a growing international population, and residential security programmes for this community are the standard operating baseline. Source: FCDO Suriname travel advice (2026); US State Dept Suriname Level 2 travel advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Port-au-Prince
item.description: Residential security in Port-au-Prince operates in one of the most challenging urban environments in the Western Hemisphere. Gang alliances control significant portions of the city, including major transit routes, and the national police force operates with severely degraded capacity. The FCDO advises against all travel to Haiti, and the US State Department classifies Haiti at Level 4 (Do Not Travel) as of 2026. Organisations maintaining a presence in Port-au-Prince do so with Haiti-specialist security providers, compound-grade protection in Petion-Ville, and tested evacuation plans. There is no standard residential security programme applicable to Port-au-Prince; every programme is built to the specific compound location and organisational risk tolerance.

item.title: Residential Security in San Salvador
item.description: Residential security in San Salvador is calibrated to a medium risk environment that has improved materially since El Salvador’s large-scale gang crackdown from 2022, but which still warrants professional planning for international executives and their families. The recommended residential zones in Colonia Escalon, Zona Rosa, Santa Elena, and San Benito provide the most appropriate combination of security infrastructure and proximity to business facilities. Source: FCDO El Salvador travel advice (2026); US State Dept El Salvador Level 3 travel advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Tegucigalpa
item.description: Residential security in Tegucigalpa addresses a persistent high-crime environment that requires structured compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning for any international organisation or individual based in the city. Honduras has one of the region’s highest crime rates, with gang activity, extortion, and kidnapping as documented risks affecting expatriate residential communities. The recommended approach is based in the Lomas del Guijarro, Palmira, or Colonia San Carlos zones, with DGSEI-licensed compound guarding and systematic staff background checking. Source: FCDO Honduras travel advice (2026); US State Dept Honduras Level 3 travel advisory (2026).

item.title: Residential Security in Ulaanbaatar
item.description:
Residential security in Ulaanbaatar addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Ulaanbaatar

Expatriate residential security in Ulaanbaatar addresses petty crime including bag-snatching and pickpocketing, drink-spiking in entertainment districts, and extreme winter conditions and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Mongolia travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Ulaanbaatar

A residential security programme in Ulaanbaatar covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Vientiane
item.description:
Residential security in Vientiane addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Vientiane

Expatriate residential security in Vientiane addresses petty theft, road accidents, and the regulatory constraints of operating in a one-party state and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Laos travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Vientiane

A residential security programme in Vientiane covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Yaounde
item.description: Residential security in Yaounde is structured around MINAT-licensed operators, the Bastos diplomatic district as the recommended residential base, and systematic staff vetting for an environment that includes both city-level crime risks and the national context of active armed conflicts in other regions of Cameroon. Oil sector companies, NGOs, and diplomatic missions based in Yaounde all operate residential security programmes in Bastos, and the district’s accumulated security infrastructure makes it the appropriate foundation for any new residential programme. Source: FCDO Cameroon travel advice (2026); US State Dept Cameroon Level 3 travel advisory (2026); MINAT Cameroon.

item.title: Residential Security in Caracas
item.description: Residential security in Caracas is a serious operational requirement for any foreign national or expatriate spending extended time in the city. The KFR threat in Venezuela does not confine itself to street-level or transit operations: criminal networks conduct surveillance of residential properties, profile their occupants, and in some cases exploit inside information from domestic staff or service contractors. A residence in eastern Caracas without professional security arrangements is not merely vulnerable: it is a target that is actively assessed.

item.title: Residential Security in Kabul
item.description:
Residential security in Kabul is a compound-level operation rather than a standard guard and alarm service. The post-2021 security environment, characterised by IS-KP complex attacks, Taliban governance of all security operations, and the absence of any Western diplomatic or emergency services backstop, means that the residential security plan for any foreign national staying in Kabul must be comprehensive, self-sufficient, and independently maintained.

Compound Security as the Framework

International organisations and commercial operators with Kabul residential operations have progressively converged on the purpose-built compound model: a property with a perimeter barrier, controlled vehicular and pedestrian access, vehicle search capability, a guard force from a Taliban-authorised operator, hardened accommodation within the compound, and communications systems that function independently of the mobile network.

item.title: Residential Security in Karachi
item.description: Residential security in Karachi is a substantive operational requirement, not a precautionary measure that can be reduced as familiarity with the city grows. The districts favoured by expatriate and corporate residents, DHA, Clifton, and Bath Island, are lower-risk relative to other parts of Karachi, but they are also the areas that criminal kidnap networks specifically target because that is where the people they want to target live. Professional residential security provision is the appropriate response to this documented reality.

item.title: Residential Security in Kathmandu
item.description: Residential security in Kathmandu requires a different threat weighting from the critical-risk cities in this region. The primary concerns are not terrorism or organised crime at the level of Karachi or Kabul, but rather the seismic hazard that makes building quality a genuine residential variable, the operational disruption of bandhs, and the general household management discipline that reduces opportunistic crime risk in a city with a mixed safety profile for foreign nationals.

item.title: Residential Security in Kuwait City
item.description: Kuwait City’s residential security environment is shaped by the same factors that drive the elevated risk assessment for business travel: US State Department Level 3 advisory conditions, Iran-aligned militia activity in the wider region, disrupted domestic Islamist plots, and road safety concerns documented by FCDO as hazardous. For expatriate households, oil sector executives in residence, and HNWI principals living in Kuwait City, residential security is an ongoing operational consideration rather than a one-time precaution.

item.title: Residential Security in Panama City
item.description: Panama City’s residential security environment reflects the city’s dual identity: as Central America’s primary financial hub, it attracts HNWI residents, senior banking executives and corporate principals who require ongoing residential protection planning beyond the standard building security arrangements. The medium risk picture, carjacking on approach routes, organised crime activity in peripheral areas and periodic civil unrest, means that residential security assessment is a practical tool for corporate and HNWI households, not an overreaction.

item.title: Residential Security in San Jose
item.description: San Jose’s residential security environment is shaped by Costa Rica’s genuine stability, which distinguishes it clearly from most Central American alternatives. Escazu, the primary residential zone for multinational executives and senior corporate households, is assessed as one of the safest residential districts in Central America. A professional residential security assessment in this context is proportionate, targeted and typically focused on the gaps that even safe environments carry: vehicle break-in risk, domestic staff access management and physical property security.

item.title: Residential Security in Santiago
item.description: Santiago’s residential security environment is characterised by the same duality that marks the city’s overall risk picture: Chile is South America’s most stable major country, and Las Condes and Vitacura are among the most secure residential districts on the continent. Yet FCDO documents specific, concrete risks, including carjacking on affluent highways, vehicle targeting, and civil unrest with a predictable calendar, that a professional residential security assessment directly addresses for HNWI and corporate households.

item.title: Residential Security in Bamako
item.description:
Bamako’s residential security environment is defined by a critical terrorism threat from JNIM, a post-coup political landscape that has reshaped Mali’s security sector, and documented armed robbery risks that make villa compound living with armed guard deployment the baseline operating standard. Residential operations in the Malian capital require a programme designed for a high-threat environment, not a standard corporate relocation market.

The residential environment in Bamako

The FCDO maintains a critical-level threat designation for Mali, with terrorism as the primary driver of elevated risk. JNIM has demonstrated sustained capability for attacks across Mali, including in the capital, with locations associated with international organisations as documented targets. Mali’s August 2020 and May 2021 coups have added political uncertainty and the complicating presence of Wagner-affiliated forces to an already complex security environment.

item.title: Residential Security in Conakry
item.description:
Conakry’s residential security environment is shaped by a military junta, degraded public infrastructure, and documented armed robbery and home invasion risks that directly affect expatriate and NGO residential compounds. Structured compound security, generator resilience, and staff vetting are the three pillars of a functional residential protection programme in the Guinean capital.

The residential environment in Conakry

Guinea has been governed by the CNRD military junta since Colonel Mamadi Doumbouya seized power in September 2021. The FCDO maintains a high-risk designation for Conakry, citing political instability, crime, and the unpredictability of the security forces as principal drivers. NGO, diplomatic, and extractive-industry personnel form the core expatriate community, and almost all operate from compound-based residential arrangements rather than open residential settings.

item.title: Residential Security in Guadalajara
item.description:
Guadalajara’s residential security environment reflects the complex dynamic of a major Mexican business city with a significant CJNG cartel presence, documented kidnapping and carjacking risks for senior executives, and a developed private security sector governed by Mexico’s federal SVSP framework. Residential security for corporate and expatriate principals in Guadalajara requires a programme calibrated to the city’s specific threat profile rather than a generic corporate relocation standard.

The residential environment in Guadalajara

Guadalajara is Mexico’s second-largest city and a major corporate and technology hub, with a substantial expatriate community concentrated in Providencia and Zapopan’s gated developments. The FCDO maintains a high-risk designation for Jalisco, noting the CJNG’s presence as the dominant driver of elevated violent crime and kidnapping risk in the metropolitan area. While most CJNG activity is directed at rival criminal organisations and state security forces, the cartel’s presence shapes the threat environment for senior corporate and high-net-worth residential principals.

item.title: Residential Security in Harare
item.description:
Harare’s residential security environment reflects Zimbabwe’s prolonged economic crisis, post-Mugabe political instability, and documented armed robbery and home invasion risks that affect expatriate, NGO, and senior corporate households in the city’s northern suburbs. A structured programme combining compound hardening, ZRP-licensed static guarding, and emergency planning is the baseline for residential operations in the Zimbabwean capital.

The residential environment in Harare

The FCDO maintains a high-risk designation for Zimbabwe, citing crime and economic volatility as the primary drivers affecting residential security. Zimbabwe’s economy has experienced repeated currency crises, fuel shortages, and power supply failures that directly shape the operational environment for residential security teams. Economic hardship creates elevated opportunistic crime risk and increases the insider threat from household staff under financial pressure.

item.title: Residential Security in Islamabad
item.description:
Islamabad presents a residential security environment shaped by terrorism risk, political instability, and the practical constraints of load-shedding and an unregulated informal security market. Expatriates and diplomats based in the capital require a structured residential security programme that goes beyond a guard at the gate to address perimeter hardening, staff vetting, and emergency planning.

The residential environment in Islamabad

The FCDO maintains a high-threat designation for Pakistan, including Islamabad, with terrorism as the primary driver of elevated risk. Tehrik-i-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) has demonstrated capability for attacks in the capital, and the threat extends to residential targets associated with diplomatic and international business communities. This drives demand for residential security services well beyond what a typical corporate relocation city would require.

item.title: Residential Security in Mogadishu
item.description:
Mogadishu represents one of the most demanding residential security environments in the world for international personnel. The FCDO advises against all travel to Somalia, Al-Shabaab has directly targeted international organisation compounds, and the operating standard for all international residential operations is exclusively villa compound accommodation with 24/7 armed guard deployment, a quick-reaction force retainer, and blast-hardened infrastructure.

The residential environment in Mogadishu

Somalia’s Federal Government has maintained a fragile hold on Mogadishu since AMISOM forces established the current security perimeter, but Al-Shabaab continues to conduct regular attacks within the city. The FCDO’s critical threat designation reflects both the frequency of attacks and their deliberate targeting of international personnel and organisations. Residential operations for UN, diplomatic, and NGO principals are conducted exclusively within designated and hardened compound zones: Halane, Villa Somalia compound, and a small number of accredited NGO compounds in Hodan and Wadajir districts.

item.title: Residential Security in Naypyidaw
item.description:
Naypyidaw presents one of the most restricted residential operating environments in the world for international personnel. The SAC military junta controls all aspects of residential security licensing, movement, and compound access, and the FCDO advises against all but essential travel to Myanmar. Operational planning must begin with evacuation rather than normalisation.

The residential environment in Naypyidaw

Myanmar has been governed by the State Administration Council (SAC) since the February 2021 coup that ousted the elected government. The FCDO maintains a critical-level threat designation for Myanmar as a whole, reflecting ongoing armed conflict between SAC forces and resistance movements, arbitrary detention of international nationals, and the complete absence of rule-of-law protections. Naypyidaw, as the military capital, sits under direct SAC security oversight.

item.title: Residential Security in Niamey
item.description:
Niamey’s residential security environment is defined by a critical terrorism threat from JNIM and ISIS-Sahel, a post-coup political landscape that has affected the operating conditions for international security providers, and documented kidnapping risk that makes compound-based living with armed guard deployment and tested emergency planning the baseline for all international residential operations in the Nigerien capital.

The residential environment in Niamey

Niger has been governed by the CNSP military junta since Colonel Abdourahamane Tchiani seized power in July 2023. The coup expelled French military forces, suspended cooperation with several Western partners, and created significant uncertainty about the conditions under which international organisations and their personnel can operate. The FCDO maintains a critical-level threat designation for Niger, citing terrorism, kidnapping, and political instability.

item.title: Residential Security in Port of Spain
item.description: Port of Spain’s residential security environment is shaped by some of the highest violent crime rates in the Caribbean, driven by gang warfare and firearms crime, combined with documented home invasion and carjacking risks that affect the city’s affluent northern and western suburbs where energy-sector and corporate expatriates are concentrated. A structured programme combining compound hardening, PSARA-licensed guard deployment, and a monitored alarm connection to an armed response company is the baseline for residential operations in the Trinidadian capital.

item.title: Residential Security in Tripoli
item.description:
Tripoli represents one of the highest-risk residential security environments globally for international personnel. The FCDO advises against all travel to most parts of Libya, including noting a very high kidnapping risk, inter-faction armed conflict in residential districts, and terrorism. Villa compound living with armed guard deployment, hardened perimeter infrastructure, and tested evacuation planning is the only appropriate residential model for oil-sector and diplomatic personnel in the Libyan capital.

The residential environment in Tripoli

Libya has experienced fragmented governance and armed conflict since 2011, and Tripoli’s residential environment for international personnel remains shaped by the competing authority of the Government of National Unity, multiple armed factions, and the underlying risk of inter-faction conflict that has erupted in residential districts on multiple occasions. The FCDO’s critical threat designation reflects both the kidnapping risk and the conflict environment.

item.title: Residential Security in Dakar
item.description:
Dakar is West Africa’s most stable democratic capital, with a residential security environment that reflects a medium-risk profile rather than the high-risk compound model required in Lagos, Kinshasa, or Luanda. The residential programme in Dakar focuses on property quality, staff vetting, zone awareness, and Sahel terrorism context briefing rather than high-profile security infrastructure.

The residential environment in Dakar

Dakar’s primary expatriate and HNWI residential zones on the Cap-Vert peninsula, Almadies, Mermoz, and the embassy district provide a comparatively manageable residential environment by sub-Saharan African standards. Crime affecting expatriate properties is documented but the risk profile does not require the armed compound architecture standard in higher-risk West African cities. The Sahel terrorism regional context and the periodic political protest cycles that Dakar has experienced require situational awareness rather than fortress-style residential security.

item.title: Residential Security in Douala
item.description:
Residential security in Douala addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Douala

Compound-based residential arrangements are standard for expatriates in Douala, reflecting the high threat environment. Structured compound security, infrastructure resilience, and staff vetting are the foundations of a functional residential protection programme. Source: FCDO Cameroon travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Douala

A residential security programme in Douala covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Erbil
item.description:
Residential security in Erbil addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Erbil

Compound-based residential arrangements are standard for expatriates in Erbil, reflecting the high threat environment. Structured compound security, infrastructure resilience, and staff vetting are the foundations of a functional residential protection programme. Source: FCDO Iraq travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Erbil

A residential security programme in Erbil covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Jeddah
item.description:
Residential security in Jeddah addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Jeddah

Compound-based residential arrangements are standard for expatriates in Jeddah, reflecting the high threat environment. Structured compound security, infrastructure resilience, and staff vetting are the foundations of a functional residential protection programme. Source: FCDO Saudi Arabia travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Jeddah

A residential security programme in Jeddah covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Kano
item.description:
Residential security in Kano addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Kano

Compound-based residential arrangements are standard for expatriates in Kano, reflecting the high threat environment. Structured compound security, infrastructure resilience, and staff vetting are the foundations of a functional residential protection programme. Source: FCDO Nigeria travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Kano

A residential security programme in Kano covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Kigali
item.description:
Kigali’s residential security programme reflects Rwanda’s status as one of East Africa’s most stable and well-governed cities. The residential programme focuses on domestic staff vetting, property survey improvements, digital privacy considerations for politically exposed principals, and emergency protocol establishment rather than the high-intensity compound security model required in higher-risk cities.

The residential environment in Kigali

Kigali consistently records among the lowest street crime rates in East Africa. The Rwanda National Police maintains a visible and professional presence, and Rwanda’s governance framework is reflected in the comparatively orderly functioning of the private security sector. The residential security considerations that remain relevant are domestic staff vetting (which is necessary regardless of external crime levels), property-specific vulnerabilities in a rapidly developing city with varied property stock, and the specific surveillance and political environment considerations that affect diplomatically or politically exposed principals.

item.title: Residential Security in Kinshasa
item.description:
Kinshasa residential security is calibrated to the DRC’s operating environment: compound-based accommodation in Gombe, Ngaliema, or Lingwala, Ministry of Interior-licensed operators, and infrastructure-resilience measures for a city where power supply cannot be assumed.

The residential environment in Kinshasa

Kinshasa is sub-Saharan Africa’s second-largest city and the entry point for the DRC’s mining, oil, and extractives sectors. The residential environment for senior expatriates and HNWI principals is manageable within the compound model, but several variables are non-negotiable: generator backup for security systems, domestic staff vetting, armed response availability, and a medevac protocol that does not rely on Kinshasa’s limited hospital infrastructure for serious trauma.

item.title: Residential Security in Lahore
item.description:
Residential security in Lahore addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Lahore

Compound-based residential arrangements are standard for expatriates in Lahore, reflecting the high threat environment. Structured compound security, infrastructure resilience, and staff vetting are the foundations of a functional residential protection programme. Source: FCDO Pakistan travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Lahore

A residential security programme in Lahore covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Lima
item.description:
Lima residential security is calibrated to Peru’s urban crime environment: SUCAMEC-licensed operators delivering property surveys, domestic staff vetting, and vehicle crime protocols for expatriate and HNWI families in Miraflores, San Isidro, and La Molina.

The residential environment in Lima

Lima’s crime environment produces a residential security programme focused on vehicle crime, domestic staff management, and the specific express kidnapping risk associated with unlicensed transport. The Miraflores-San Isidro corridor provides the most manageable residential zone in the city, but the movement patterns associated with family daily life, including school runs, shopping, and social activity, extend beyond this corridor and require professional planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Luanda
item.description:
Luanda residential security is calibrated to Angola’s oil sector operating standard: compound-based accommodation in the Talatona, Miramar, and Alvalade zones, Ministry of Interior-licensed security operators, and emergency protocols that account for Luanda’s infrastructure environment including power unreliability and the distance from international-standard medical care.

The residential environment in Luanda

Armed robbery and vehicle crime are documented across Luanda, with higher concentration in areas outside the principal expatriate zones. The expatriate residential security model in Angola has been shaped by decades of oil sector operations: secured compound accommodation, vetted transport, and a security framework that addresses both criminal and infrastructure risk variables. The Ministry of Interior licensing requirement for all security operators is the regulatory baseline; operational quality within that framework varies substantially by provider.

item.title: Residential Security in Lusaka
item.description:
Lusaka residential security is calibrated to Zambia’s medium-risk profile and the well-developed ZSIRA regulatory framework: licensed operators delivering property surveys, domestic staff vetting, armed response assessment, and vehicle crime protocols for expatriate and mining sector families in Kabulonga and Woodlands.

The residential environment in Lusaka

Lusaka’s medium-risk profile reflects Zambia’s comparatively stable political environment and lower violent crime rates than its neighbours to the north (DRC) and east (Mozambique). The residential security programme focuses on property survey quality, domestic staff vetting, armed response provider assessment, and the specific vehicle crime risks associated with Lusaka’s low-density residential areas and isolated approach roads.

item.title: Residential Security in Manama
item.description:
Residential security in Manama addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Manama

Expatriate residential security in Manama addresses crime risk and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Bahrain travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Manama

A residential security programme in Manama covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Maputo
item.description:
Maputo residential security is calibrated to Mozambique’s high-risk profile and the energy sector operating framework that has shaped the Maputo expatriate security model: Ministry of Interior-licensed operators delivering compound security assessments, domestic staff vetting, armed robbery protocols, and medevac-ready emergency planning.

The residential environment in Maputo

Maputo’s residential environment for senior expatriates and energy sector personnel centres on the Sommerschield and Polana Cimento zones, which provide the most consistent security infrastructure and services in the city. Armed robbery and vehicle crime are documented across Maputo’s zones; the residential programme addresses these through property assessment, vetted transport protocols, and armed response integration.

item.title: Residential Security in Medellin
item.description:
Medellin residential security is calibrated to Colombia’s regulatory framework and the city’s specific crime environment: Supervigilancia-licensed operators delivering property surveys, domestic staff vetting with organised crime awareness, and extortion protocol briefing for El Poblado and Laureles-based expatriate and HNWI families.

The residential environment in Medellin

Medellin has undergone significant transformation since the 1990s, and the El Poblado zone where most senior expatriate and HNWI residential accommodation is concentrated provides a comparatively manageable security environment within Colombia’s broader high-risk national picture. Organised crime’s primary residential security relevance is through vetting and intelligence risk rather than direct violence targeting international residents; the extortion risk is more material than the physical robbery risk for this demographic.

item.title: Residential Security in Phnom Penh
item.description:
Residential security in Phnom Penh addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Phnom Penh

Expatriate residential security in Phnom Penh addresses crime risk and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Cambodia travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Phnom Penh

A residential security programme in Phnom Penh covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Port Harcourt
item.description:
Residential security in Port Harcourt addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Port Harcourt

Compound-based residential arrangements are standard for expatriates in Port Harcourt, reflecting the high threat environment. Structured compound security, infrastructure resilience, and staff vetting are the foundations of a functional residential protection programme. Source: FCDO Nigeria travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Port Harcourt

A residential security programme in Port Harcourt covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Quito
item.description:
Residential security in Quito addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Quito

Compound-based residential arrangements are standard for expatriates in Quito, reflecting the high threat environment. Structured compound security, infrastructure resilience, and staff vetting are the foundations of a functional residential protection programme. Source: FCDO Ecuador travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Quito

A residential security programme in Quito covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Santo Domingo
item.description:
Residential security in Santo Domingo addresses the compound protection, staff vetting, and emergency planning requirements of expatriates, NGO personnel, and diplomats based in the city.

The residential environment in Santo Domingo

Expatriate residential security in Santo Domingo addresses crime risk and the need for reliable emergency response arrangements. Source: FCDO Dominican Republic travel advice (2026).

What residential security covers in Santo Domingo

A residential security programme in Santo Domingo covers static compound guarding, vehicle access control, perimeter and CCTV maintenance, domestic staff vetting, and emergency response coordination including medevac planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Tunis
item.description:
Tunis residential security is calibrated to Tunisia’s medium-risk profile and specific terrorism context: Ministry of Interior-licensed operators delivering property surveys, domestic staff vetting, terrorism soft-target assessment, and residential zone guidance for expatriate and HNWI families in Les Berges du Lac, La Marsa, and Carthage.

The residential environment in Tunis

Tunis has a comparatively manageable residential security environment for North Africa, with an active private security sector, functioning civil identity registry, and a coastal residential corridor that provides the best available infrastructure and services. The documented terrorism risk, rooted in Tunisia’s attack history and the ongoing Libya border situation, is the primary residential security consideration that distinguishes Tunis from lower-risk Mediterranean capitals.

item.title: Residential Security in Yangon
item.description:
Yangon residential security operates under Myanmar’s post-coup political and security framework: essential-presence organisations and corporate businesses maintaining a Yangon presence require a residential programme calibrated to detention risk, digital surveillance, and evacuation planning in addition to the standard residential security elements.

The residential environment in Yangon

Yangon remains comparatively stable relative to the active conflict zones in Myanmar’s peripheral states, but the military government’s surveillance capability, arbitrary detention record, and the general deterioration of civil liberties since 2021 create a residential security environment unlike any other major city in Southeast Asia. FCDO and the US State Department both assess Myanmar at their highest risk level; residential postings that continue are managed as essential-presence decisions with full awareness of the elevated risk.

item.title: Residential Security in Bangalore
item.description: Bangalore is the centre of India’s technology economy and hosts a large and growing expatriate residential community of technology sector professionals, senior executives, and investment community personnel across Whitefield, Electronic City, Koramangala, and Indiranagar. FCDO rates Bangalore at low to moderate risk. The residential security requirement is proportionate to an environment where expat properties are visible targets for residential burglary, domestic staff vetting is important given the domestic labour market dynamics, and the Cauvery protest risk creates periodic access disruption that benefits from advance planning.

item.title: Residential Security in Beirut
item.description:
Beirut residential security operates at the critical-risk end of the spectrum, with specific requirements not present in most other global residential security programmes: power backup security system design, post-2020 structural vulnerability assessment, politically-aware domestic staff vetting, and a documented residential evacuation plan. Ministry of Interior-licensed operators are the regulatory foundation.

For the full Beirut threat picture and FCDO Lebanon advisory, see our Beirut city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Beirut residential programme, executive protection in Beirut covers the full Ministry of Interior-licensed personal security detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Casablanca
item.description: Casablanca is North Africa’s primary commercial centre and hosts a significant corporate and financial sector expat community across the Maarif, Gauthier, and Ain Diab residential districts. The city’s overall risk rating is low. The residential security programme addresses the specific concerns relevant to expat households: property burglary in known expat areas, domestic staff vetting in a market where verification requires structure, and legal compliance management for a jurisdiction with strict laws that affect daily household operation.

item.title: Residential Security in Colombo
item.description:
Colombo residential security serves a medium-risk city with a post-2019 terrorism-aware property assessment framework, NSC-licensed guard deployment, and standard domestic staff vetting protocols. The residential programme is substantially less complex than Beirut or Dhaka but requires specific attention to the post-Easter-bombing threat geography and the Sri Lanka medical evacuation routing.

For the full Colombo security context, see our Colombo city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Colombo residential programme, executive protection in Colombo covers the full NSC-licensed personal security detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Dar es Salaam
item.description:
Dar es Salaam residential security serves a high-risk environment with a significantly elevated perimeter and access control specification relative to peer African cities. PSRA-licensed guard deployment, specific night patrol protocols, insider-information-aware domestic staff vetting, and a documented medical evacuation protocol are the foundations of the Tanzania residential programme.

For the full Dar es Salaam security picture, see our Dar es Salaam city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential security programme in Tanzania, executive protection in Dar es Salaam covers the full PSRA-licensed close protection detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Delhi
item.description:
Delhi is India’s capital and a major destination for diplomatic missions, government-sector business, and senior corporate visits. The FCDO rates Delhi at moderate risk. Residential security for corporate assignees and expats in the capital needs to address a genuine terrorism background, a documented sexual assault risk for female principals, and a domestic staff market that requires systematic vetting to manage the fraud and disclosure risk.

PSARA-licensed Delhi state operators deliver all residential guard deployments and surveys. The Delhi city briefing provides current FCDO context, including the terrorism assessment and district-level risk notes. For principals requiring mobile close protection alongside their residential security programme, executive protection in Delhi covers the full personal security detail and CP officer deployment across the capital.

item.title: Residential Security in Dhaka
item.description:
Dhaka residential security addresses a high-risk capital with specific requirements for terrorism-adjacent venue awareness in the Gulshan zone, hartal day compound lockdown planning, apartment building access control, and enhanced domestic staff vetting for migrant household workers. Ministry of Home Affairs-registered operators are the regulatory foundation.

For the full Dhaka security picture, see our Dhaka city page. Principals requiring executive protection alongside their Dhaka residential programme should review executive protection in Dhaka for the full Ministry-licensed close protection programme.

item.title: Residential Security in Geneva
item.description: Geneva is Switzerland’s international city and one of the world’s most significant HNWI residential locations, combining private banking wealth with a diplomatic community serving the UN, WTO, WHO, and hundreds of other international organisations. The city’s ambient crime statistics are excellent. The residential security requirement for Geneva’s HNWI and diplomatic households is driven by wealth-targeting risk, the intelligence collection environment around multilateral processes, and the operational standards expected in one of the world’s most discreet and professionally managed residential security markets.

item.title: Residential Security in Ho Chi Minh City
item.description:
Ho Chi Minh City residential security serves a low-risk city with specific considerations for condominium building security infrastructure, motorbike theft prevention, and Vietnam’s Ministry of Public Security licensing constraint for all guard deployments. The residential programme is substantially less complex than Dhaka or Dar es Salaam but requires MPS-licensed operators and structured domestic staff vetting.

For the broader Ho Chi Minh City security context, see our Ho Chi Minh City page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential security programme in Vietnam, executive protection in Ho Chi Minh City covers the full MPS-licensed close protection detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Kampala
item.description:
Kampala residential security serves a high-risk East African capital with specific compound perimeter standards, night guard patrol protocols, LC1-based domestic staff vetting, and boda boda-aware compound entry management. UPF-licensed operators under the Private Security Organisations Act are the regulatory foundation.

For the full Kampala security picture including the ADF terrorism context, see our Kampala city page. Principals requiring executive protection alongside their Kampala residential programme should review executive protection in Kampala for the full UPF-licensed close protection detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Kyiv
item.description:
Kyiv residential assignments exist to support diplomatic and essential personnel who are required to maintain a presence in Ukraine’s capital during the ongoing conflict. The FCDO advises against all travel to Ukraine. The information here is for those who have determined that the assignment is unavoidable and need to reduce the risks that the environment presents.

Generator-powered accommodation with confirmed basement shelter access is the minimum property standard. Wartime-experienced operators with current Ukraine knowledge deliver all guard deployments. Every Kyiv residential assignment includes a mandatory evacuation plan built around overland transit to Poland before the assignment begins.

item.title: Residential Security in Mombasa
item.description:
Mombasa residential security serves a high-risk coastal environment with specific considerations for beach and marine perimeter management not present in inland city residential programmes. PSRA-licensed operators under Kenya’s Security Industry Act 2018 are the regulatory foundation; DCI-verified domestic staff vetting and specific night guard coastal patrol protocols complete the programme.

For the full Mombasa security context including al-Shabaab awareness, see our Mombasa city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Mombasa coastal residential programme, executive protection in Mombasa covers the full PSRA-licensed personal security detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Montreal
item.description:
Montreal is one of Canada’s principal commercial cities with a significant HNWI residential community across Westmount, Outremont, and Mont-Royal. Canada’s overall stability means the residential security environment is low risk. The specific concerns for Montreal residential assignments are HNWI wealth-targeting risk, the foreign state interference environment documented by CSIS, and the operational requirements of running residential security through a demanding Montreal winter.

BSPCE-licensed operators with bilingual French-English capability deliver all Montreal residential guard deployments and surveys. Winter cold-weather protocols are a standard element of all deployments running between November and March. The Montreal city briefing provides current CSIS foreign interference advisory context and the BSPCE regulatory framework. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their residential programme, executive protection in Montreal covers the HNWI event security and bilingual CP officer capability across the city.

item.title: Residential Security in Muscat
item.description:
Muscat residential security serves one of the Gulf’s lowest-risk residential environments. The primary considerations are ROP-licensed guard deployment, Omani domestic worker vetting through the TAQDEER platform and ROP clearance, and Al Mouj marina property-specific perimeter management for HNWI residents. The overall security specification is lower than Gulf peers with higher crime profiles.

For the broader Muscat and Oman security context, see our Muscat city page. For HNWI principals requiring executive protection alongside their Muscat residential programme, executive protection in Muscat covers the full ROP-licensed personal security detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Naples
item.description: Naples is one of Italy’s most distinctive cities and the gateway to one of the world’s most desirable coastal regions, with HNWI demand for residential properties in Chiaia, Posillipo, Vomero, and the Amalfi coastal villages. The residential security programme for Naples addresses the specific concerns that distinguish it from northern Italian cities: the Camorra’s influence in local commercial and labour markets requiring more thorough contractor and staff vetting, scooter-borne theft and vehicle security, and the extension of the residential programme to coastal Campania where different access and emergency protocols apply.

item.title: Residential Security in Rome
item.description: Rome is Italy’s capital and the location of the Vatican, hosting a significant diplomatic and HNWI residential community across Parioli, EUR, and the Aventino hill. The city’s ambient security environment is manageable. The residential security programme for diplomatic and HNWI households addresses the specific concerns relevant to these profiles: residential burglary and vehicle targeting in the Parioli area, organised pickpocket awareness for household staff and guests, and ZTL zone vehicle management for properties with restricted access considerations.

item.title: Residential Security in Shanghai
item.description: Shanghai is China’s commercial capital and hosts one of the world’s largest expat residential communities of technology, finance, and professional services assignees in the Jing’an, French Concession, and Pudong areas. The physical crime profile in expat residential areas is low. The residential security programme for Shanghai assignees focuses on domestic staff vetting, digital security in an environment where sensitive commercial activity operates within a state-monitored infrastructure, and legal risk briefing covering the exit ban framework that can affect assignees with significant commercial activity in China.

item.title: Residential Security in Tashkent
item.description:
Tashkent residential security addresses a medium-risk capital with specific requirements not typical of most cities: SNB-aware guard personnel vetting to protect household confidentiality, Soviet-era building lobby security upgrades, and a communications security component reflecting the active intelligence monitoring environment. MVD-licensed operators are the regulatory foundation.

For the broader Tashkent and Uzbekistan security context, see our Tashkent city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Tashkent residential programme, executive protection in Tashkent covers the full MVD-licensed close protection detail.

item.title: Residential Security in Tbilisi
item.description:
Tbilisi residential security serves a medium-risk city with specific considerations for historic building access control in the Old Town, political protest proximity in the central districts, and the Georgian Ministry of Internal Affairs licensing framework. The overall security specification is lower than high-risk East African or South Asian cities but requires specific attention to Tbilisi’s distinct residential property stock and political context.

For the full Tbilisi security picture, see our Tbilisi city page. For principals requiring executive protection alongside their Tbilisi residential programme, executive protection in Tbilisi covers the full Ministry-licensed personal security detail across Georgia.

item.title: Residential Security in Warsaw
item.description:
